translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of that one, who Hath not walked in the counsel of the wicked. And in the way of sinners hath not stood, And in the seat of scorners hath not sat;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But -- in the law of Jehovah [is] his delight, And in His law he doth meditate by day and by night:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath been as a tree, Planted by rivulets of water, That giveth its fruit in its season, And its leaf doth not wither, And all that he doth he causeth to prosper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not so the wicked: But -- as chaff that wind driveth away!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore the wicked rise not in judgment, Nor sinners in the company of the righteous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah is knowing the way of the righteous, And the way of the wicked is lost!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why have nations tumultuously assembled? And do peoples meditate vanity?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Station themselves do kings of the earth, And princes have been united together, Against Jehovah, and against His Messiah:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Let us draw off Their cords, And cast from us Their thick bands.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is sitting in the heavens doth laugh, The Lord doth mock at them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then doth He speak unto them in His anger, And in His wrath He doth trouble them:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'And I -- I have anointed My King, Upon Zion -- My holy hill.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I declare concerning a statute: Jehovah said unto me, 'My Son Thou [art], I to-day have brought thee forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ask of Me and I give nations -- thy inheritance, And thy possession -- the ends of earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost rule them with a sceptre of iron, As a vessel of a potter Thou dost crush them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, O kings, act wisely, Be instructed, O judges of earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Serve ye Jehovah with fear, And rejoice with trembling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Kiss the Chosen One, lest He be angry, And ye lose the way, When His anger burneth but a little, O the happiness of all trusting in Him!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David, in his fleeing from the face of Absalom his son. Jehovah, how have my distresses multiplied! Many are rising up against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many are saying of my soul, 'There is no salvation for him in God.' Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, O Jehovah, [art] a shield for me, My honour, and lifter up of my head.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My voice [is] unto Jehovah: I call: And He answereth me from his holy hill, Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I have lain down, and I sleep, I have waked, for Jehovah sustaineth me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I am not afraid of myriads of people, That round about they have set against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rise, O Jehovah! save me, my God. Because Thou hast smitten All mine enemies [on] the cheek. The teeth of the wicked Thou hast broken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of Jehovah [is] this salvation; On Thy people [is] Thy blessing! Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer with Stringed Instruments. -- A Psalm of David. In my calling answer Thou me, O God of my righteousness. In adversity Thou gavest enlargement to me; Favour me, and hear my prayer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of men! till when [is] my glory for shame? Ye love a vain thing, ye seek a lie. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And know ye that Jehovah Hath separated a saintly one to Himself. Jehovah heareth in my calling to Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Tremble ye, and do not sin;' Say ye [thus] in your heart on your bed, And be ye silent. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sacrifice ye sacrifices of righteousness, And trust ye unto Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many are saying, 'Who doth show us good?' Lift on us the light of Thy face, O Jehovah,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast given joy in my heart, From the time their corn and their wine Have been multiplied.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In peace together I lie down and sleep, For Thou, O Jehovah, alone, In confidence dost cause me to dwell!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, 'Concerning the Inheritances.' -- A Psalm of David. My sayings hear, O Jehovah, Consider my meditation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be attentive to the voice of my cry, My king and my God, For unto Thee I pray habitually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, [at] morning Thou hearest my voice, [At] morning I set in array for Thee, And I look out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For not a God desiring wickedness [art] Thou, Evil inhabiteth Thee not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The boastful station not themselves before Thine eyes: Thou hast hated all working iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou destroyest those speaking lies, A man of blood and deceit Jehovah doth abominate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, in the abundance of Thy kindness, I enter Thy house, I bow myself toward Thy holy temple in Thy fear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, lead me in Thy righteousness, Because of those observing me, Make straight before me Thy way,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For there is no stability in their mouth. Their heart [is] mischiefs, An open grave [is] their throat, Their tongue they make smooth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Declare them guilty, O God, Let them fall from their own counsels, In the abundance of their transgressions Drive them away, Because they have rebelled against Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And rejoice do all trusting in Thee, To the age they sing, and Thou coverest them over, And those loving Thy name exult in Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou blessest the righteous, O Jehovah, As a buckler with favour dost compass him!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer with stringed instruments, on the octave. -- A Psalm of David. O Jehovah, in Thine anger reprove me not, Nor in Thy fury chastise me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Favour me, O Jehovah, for I [am] weak, Heal me, O Jehovah, For troubled have been my bones,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my soul hath been troubled greatly, And Thou, O Jehovah, till when?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn back, O Jehovah, draw out my soul, Save me for Thy kindness' sake.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For there is not in death Thy memorial, In Sheol, who doth give thanks to Thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been weary with my sighing, I meditate through all the night [on] my bed, With my tear my couch I waste.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Old from provocation is mine eye, It is old because of all mine adversaries,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn from me all ye workers of iniquity, For Jehovah heard the voice of my weeping,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah hath heard my supplication, Jehovah my prayer receiveth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ashamed and troubled greatly are all mine enemies, They turn back -- ashamed [in] a moment!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'The Erring One,' by David, that he sung to Jehovah concerning the words of Cush a Benjamite. O Jehovah, my God, in Thee I have trusted, Save me from all my pursuers, and deliver me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lest he tear as a lion my soul, Rending, and there is no deliverer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, my God, if I have done this, If there is iniquity in my hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have done my well-wisher evil, And draw mine adversary without cause,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An enemy pursueth my soul, and overtaketh, And treadeth down to the earth my life, And my honour placeth in the dust. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rise, O Jehovah, in Thine anger, Be lifted up at the wrath of mine adversaries, And awake Thou for me: Judgment Thou hast commanded:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a company of peoples compass Thee, And over it on high turn Thou back,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth judge the peoples; Judge me, O Jehovah, According to my righteousness, And according to mine integrity on me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let, I pray Thee be ended the evil of the wicked, And establish Thou the righteous, And a trier of hearts and reins is the righteous God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My shield [is] on God, Saviour of the upright in heart!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God [is] a righteous judge, And He is not angry at all times.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If [one] turn not, His sword he sharpeneth, His bow he hath trodden -- He prepareth it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, for him He hath prepared Instruments of death, His arrows for burning pursuers He maketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, he travaileth [with] iniquity, And he hath conceived perverseness, And hath brought forth falsehood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A pit he hath prepared, and he diggeth it, And he falleth into a ditch he maketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Return doth his perverseness on his head, And on his crown his violence cometh down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I thank Jehovah, According to His righteousness, And praise the name of Jehovah Most High!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, 'On the Gittith.' A Psalm of David. Jehovah, our Lord, How honourable Thy name in all the earth! Who settest thine honour on the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the mouths of infants and sucklings Thou hast founded strength, Because of Thine adversaries, To still an enemy and a self-avenger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I see Thy heavens, a work of Thy fingers, Moon and stars that Thou didst establish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] man that Thou rememberest him? The son of man that Thou inspectest him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And causest him to lack a little of Godhead, And with honour and majesty compassest him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost cause him to rule Over the works of Thy hands, All Thou hast placed under his feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sheep and oxen, all of them, And also beasts of the field,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bird of the heavens, and fish of the sea, Passing through the paths of the seas!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, our Lord, How honourable Thy name in all the earth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, 'On the Death of Labben.' -- A Psalm of David. I confess, O Jehovah, with all my heart, I recount all Thy wonders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I rejoice and exult in Thee, I praise Thy Name, O Most High.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In mine enemies turning backward, they stumble and perish from Thy face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast done my judgment and my right. Thou hast sat on a throne, A judge of righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast rebuked nations, Thou hast destroyed the wicked, Their name Thou hast blotted out to the age and for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O thou Enemy, Finished have been destructions for ever, As to cities thou hast plucked up, Perished hath their memorial with them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah to the age abideth, He is preparing for judgment His throne.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He judgeth the world in righteousness, He judgeth the peoples in uprightness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah is a tower for the bruised, A tower for times of adversity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They trust in Thee who do know Thy name, For Thou hast not forsaken Those seeking Thee, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing ye praise to Jehovah, inhabiting Zion, Declare ye among the peoples His acts,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He who is seeking for blood Them hath remembered, He hath not forgotten the cry of the afflicted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Favour me, O Jehovah, See mine affliction by those hating me, Thou who liftest me up from the gates of death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that I recount all Thy praise, In the gates of the daughter of Zion. I rejoice on Thy salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sunk have nations in a pit they made, In a net that they hid hath their foot been captured.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah hath been known, Judgment He hath done, By a work of his hands Hath the wicked been snared. Meditation. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked do turn back to Sheol, All nations forgetting God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For not for ever is the needy forgotten, The hope of the humble lost to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rise, O Jehovah, let not man be strong, Let nations be judged before Thy face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Appoint, O Jehovah, a director to them, Let nations know they [are] men! Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why, Jehovah, dost Thou stand at a distance? Thou dost hide in times of adversity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Through the pride of the wicked, Is the poor inflamed, They are caught in devices that they devised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because the wicked hath boasted Of the desire of his soul, And a dishonest gainer he hath blessed, He hath despised Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked according to the height of his face, inquireth not. 'God is not!' [are] all his devices.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pain do his ways at all times, On high [are] Thy judgments before him, All his adversaries -- he puffeth at them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath said in his heart, 'I am not moved,' To generation and generation not in evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of oaths his mouth is full, And deceits, and fraud: Under his tongue [is] perverseness and iniquity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth sit in an ambush of the villages, In secret places he doth slay the innocent. His eyes for the afflicted watch secretly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He lieth in wait in a secret place, as a lion in a covert. He lieth in wait to catch the poor, He catcheth the poor, drawing him into his net.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is bruised -- he boweth down, Fallen by his mighty ones hath the afflicted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He said in his heart, 'God hath forgotten, He hath hid His face, He hath never seen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Arise, O Jehovah! O God, lift up Thy hand! Forget not the humble.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore hath the wicked despised God? He hath said in his heart, 'It is not required.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast seen, For Thou perverseness and anger beholdest; By giving into Thy hand, On Thee doth the afflicted leave [it], Of the fatherless Thou hast been an helper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Break the arm of the wicked and the evil, Seek out his wickedness, find none;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] king to the age, and for ever, The nations have perished out of His land!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The desire of the humble Thou hast heard, O Jehovah. Thou preparest their heart; Thou causest Thine ear to attend,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To judge the fatherless and bruised: He addeth no more to oppress -- man of the earth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By David. In Jehovah I trusted, how say ye to my soul, 'They moved [to] Thy mountain for the bird?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For lo, the wicked tread a bow, They have prepared their arrow on the string, To shoot in darkness at the upright in heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When the foundations are destroyed, The righteous -- what hath he done?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Jehovah [is] in his holy temple: Jehovah -- in the heavens [is] His throne. His eyes see -- His eyelids try the sons of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah the righteous doth try. And the wicked and the lover of violence, Hath His soul hated,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He poureth on the wicked snares, fire, and brimstone, And a horrible wind [is] the portion of their cup.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For righteous [is] Jehovah, Righteousness He hath loved, The upright doth His countenance see!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, on the octave. -- A Psalm of David. Save, Jehovah, for the saintly hath failed, For the stedfast have ceased From the sons of men:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Vanity they speak each with his neighbour, Lip of flattery! With heart and heart they speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth cut off all lips of flattery, A tongue speaking great things,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who said, 'By our tongue we do mightily: Our lips [are] our own; who [is] lord over us?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of the spoiling of the poor, Because of the groaning of the needy, Now do I arise, saith Jehovah, I set in safety [him who] doth breathe for it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sayings of Jehovah [are] pure sayings; Silver tried in a furnace of earth refined sevenfold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, O Jehovah, dost preserve them, Thou keepest us from this generation to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Around the wicked walk continually, According as vileness is exalted by sons of men!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. Till when, O Jehovah, Dost Thou forget me? -- for ever? Till when dost Thou hide Thy face from me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when do I set counsels in my soul? Sorrow in my heart daily? Till when is mine enemy exalted over me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look attentively; Answer me, O Jehovah, my God, Enlighten mine eyes, lest I sleep in death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lest mine enemy say, 'I overcame him,' Mine adversaries joy when I am moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, in Thy kindness I have trusted, Rejoice doth my heart in Thy salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I do sing to Jehovah, For He hath conferred benefits upon me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By David. A fool hath said in his heart, 'God is not;' They have done corruptly, They have done abominable actions, There is not a doer of good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah from the heavens Hath looked on the sons of men, To see if there is a wise one -- seeking God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The whole have turned aside, Together they have been filthy: There is not a doer of good, not even one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Have all working iniquity not known? Those consuming my people have eaten bread, Jehovah they have not called.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There they have feared a fear, For God [is] in the generation of the righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The counsel of the poor ye cause to stink, Because Jehovah [is] his refuge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who doth give from Zion the salvation of Israel? When Jehovah doth turn back [To] a captivity of His people, Jacob doth rejoice -- Israel is glad!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David. Jehovah, who doth sojourn in Thy tent? Who doth dwell in Thy holy hill?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is walking uprightly, And working righteousness, And speaking truth in his heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath not slandered by his tongue, He hath not done to his friend evil; And reproach he hath not lifted up Against his neighbour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Despised in his eyes [is] a rejected one, And those fearing Jehovah he doth honour. He hath sworn to suffer evil, and changeth not;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His silver he hath not given in usury, And a bribe against the innocent Hath not taken; Whoso is doing these is not moved to the age!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Secret Treasure of David. Preserve me, O God, for I did trust in Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast said to Jehovah, 'My Lord Thou [art];' My good [is] not for thine own sake;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the holy ones who [are] in the land, And the honourable, all my delight [is] in them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Multiplied are their griefs, [Who] have hastened backward; I pour not out their libations of blood, Nor do I take up their names on my lips.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] the portion of my share, and of my cup, Thou -- Thou dost uphold my lot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lines have fallen to me in pleasant places, Yea, a beauteous inheritance [is] for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I bless Jehovah who hath counselled me; Also [in] the nights my reins instruct me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I did place Jehovah before me continually, Because -- at my right hand I am not moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore hath my heart been glad, And my honour doth rejoice, Also my flesh dwelleth confidently:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou dost not leave my soul to Sheol, Nor givest thy saintly one to see corruption.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou causest me to know the path of life; Fulness of joys [is] with Thy presence, Pleasant things by Thy right hand for ever!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Prayer of David. Hear, O Jehovah, righteousness, attend my cry, Give ear [to] my prayer, without lips of deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From before thee my judgment doth go out; Thine eyes do see uprightly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast proved my heart, Thou hast inspected by night, Thou hast tried me, Thou findest nothing; My thoughts pass not over my mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to doings of man, Through a word of Thy lips I have observed The paths of a destroyer;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To uphold my goings in Thy paths, My steps have not slidden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I called Thee, for Thou dost answer me, O God, incline Thine ear to me, hear my speech.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Separate wonderfully Thy kindness, O Saviour of the confiding, By Thy right hand, from withstanders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Keep me as the apple, the daughter of the eye; In shadow of Thy wings thou dost hide me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the face of the wicked who spoiled me. Mine enemies in soul go round against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their fat they have closed up, Their mouths have spoken with pride:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Our steps now have compassed [him];' Their eyes they set to turn aside in the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His likeness as a lion desirous to tear, As a young lion dwelling in secret places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Arise, O Jehovah, go before his face, Cause him to bend. Deliver my soul from the wicked, Thy sword,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From men, Thy hand, O Jehovah, From men of the world, their portion [is] in life, And [with] Thy hidden things Thou fillest their belly, They are satisfied [with] sons; And have left their abundance to their sucklings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- in righteousness, I see Thy face; I am satisfied, in awaking, [with] Thy form!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By a servant of Jehovah, by David, who hath spoken to Jehovah the words of this song in the day Jehovah delivered him from the hand of all his enemies, and from the hand of Saul, and he saith: -- I love Thee, O Jehovah, my strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] my rock, and my bulwark, And my deliverer, My God [is] my rock, I trust in Him: My shield, and a horn of my salvation, My high tower.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The 'Praised One' I call Jehovah, And from my enemies I am saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Compassed me have cords of death, And streams of the worthless make me afraid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cords of Sheol have surrounded me, Before me have been snares of death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In mine adversity I call Jehovah, And unto my God I cry. He heareth from His temple my voice, And My cry before Him cometh into His ears.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And shake and tremble doth the earth, And foundations of hills are troubled, And they shake -- because He hath wrath.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gone up hath smoke by His nostrils, And fire from His mouth consumeth, Coals have been kindled by it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He inclineth the heavens, and cometh down, And thick darkness [is] under His feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He rideth on a cherub, and doth fly, And He flieth on wings of wind.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He maketh darkness His secret place, Round about Him His tabernacle, Darkness of waters, thick clouds of the skies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the brightness over-against Him His thick clouds have passed on, Hail and coals of fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thunder in the heavens doth Jehovah, And the Most High giveth forth His voice, Hail and coals of fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He sendeth His arrows and scattereth them, And much lightning, and crusheth them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And seen are the streams of waters, And revealed are foundations of the earth. From Thy rebuke, O Jehovah, From the breath of the spirit of Thine anger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He sendeth from above -- He taketh me, He draweth me out of many waters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He delivereth me from my strong enemy, And from those hating me, For they have been stronger than I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They go before me in a day of my calamity And Jehovah is for a support to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He bringeth me forth to a large place, He draweth me out, because He delighted in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth recompense me According to my righteousness, According to the cleanness of my hands, He doth return to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have kept the ways of Jehovah, And have not done wickedly against my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For all His judgments [are] before me, And His statutes I turn not from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I am perfect with him, And I keep myself from mine iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah doth return to me, According to my righteousness, According to the cleanness of my hands, Over-against his eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With the kind Thou showest Thyself kind, With a perfect man showest Thyself perfect.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With the pure Thou showest Thyself pure, And with the perverse showest Thyself a wrestler,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou a poor people savest, And the eyes of the high causest to fall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou -- Thou lightest my lamp, Jehovah my God enlighteneth my darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For by Thee I run -- a troop! And by my God I leap a wall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God! perfect [is] His way, The saying of Jehovah is tried, A shield [is] He to all those trusting in Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For who [is] God besides Jehovah? And who [is] a rock save our God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God! who is girding me [with] strength, And He maketh perfect my way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Making my feet like hinds, And on my high places causeth me to stand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Teaching my hands for battle, And a bow of brass was brought down by my arms.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou givest to me the shield of Thy salvation, And Thy right hand doth support me, And Thy lowliness maketh me great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou enlargest my step under me, And mine ankles have not slidden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I pursue mine enemies, and overtake them, And turn back not till they are consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I smite them, and they are not able to rise, They fall under my feet,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou girdest me [with] strength for battle, Causest my withstanders to bow under me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to mine enemies -- Thou hast given to me the neck, As to those hating me -- I cut them off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They cry, and there is no saviour, On Jehovah, and He doth not answer them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I beat them as dust before wind, As mire of the streets I empty them out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost deliver me From the strivings of the people, Thou placest me for a head of nations, A people I have not known do serve me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At the hearing of the ear they hearken to me, Sons of a stranger feign obedience to me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of a stranger fade away, And are slain out of their close places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah liveth -- and blessed [is] my rock, And exalted is the God of my salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God -- who is giving vengeance to me, And He subdueth peoples under me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My deliverer from mine enemies, Above my withstanders Thou raisest me, From a man of violence dost deliver me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore I confess Thee among nations, O Jehovah, And to Thy name I sing praise,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Magnifying the salvation of His king, And doing kindness to His anointed, To David, and to his seed -- unto the age!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. The heavens [are] recounting the honour of God, And the work of His hands The expanse [is] declaring.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Day to day uttereth speech, And night to night sheweth knowledge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is no speech, and there are no words. Their voice hath not been heard.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Into all the earth hath their line gone forth, And to the end of the world their sayings, For the sun He placed a tent in them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, as a bridegroom, goeth out from his covering, He rejoiceth as a mighty one To run the path.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the end of the heavens [is] his going out, And his revolution [is] unto their ends, And nothing is hid from his heat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The law of Jehovah [is] perfect, refreshing the soul, The testimonies of Jehovah [are] stedfast, Making wise the simple,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The precepts of Jehovah [are] upright, Rejoicing the heart, The command of Jehovah [is] pure, enlightening the eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The fear of Jehovah [is] clean, standing to the age, The judgments of Jehovah [are] true, They have been righteous -- together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are more desirable than gold, Yea, than much fine gold; and sweeter than honey, Even liquid honey of the comb.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- Thy servant is warned by them, 'In keeping them [is] a great reward.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Errors! who doth understand? From hidden ones declare me innocent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- from presumptuous ones keep back Thy servant, Let them not rule over me, Then am I perfect, And declared innocent of much transgression,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the sayings of my mouth, And the meditation of my heart, Be for a pleasing thing before Thee, O Jehovah, my rock, and my redeemer!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. Jehovah doth answer thee, In a day of adversity, The name of the God of Jacob doth set thee on high,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth send thy help from the sanctuary, And from Zion doth support thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth remember all thy presents, And thy burnt-offering doth reduce to ashes. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth give to thee according to thy heart, And all thy counsel doth fulfil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We sing of thy salvation, And in the name of our God set up a banner. Jehovah doth fulfil all thy requests.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Now I have known That Jehovah hath saved His anointed, He answereth him from His holy heavens, With the saving might of His right hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Some of chariots, and some of horses, And we of the name of Jehovah our God Make mention.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They -- they have bowed and have fallen, And we have risen and station ourselves upright.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, save the king, He doth answer us in the day we call!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. Jehovah, in Thy strength is the king joyful, In Thy salvation how greatly he rejoiceth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The desire of his heart Thou gavest to him, And the request of his lips Thou hast not withheld. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou puttest before him blessings of goodness, Thou settest on his head a crown of fine gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Life he hath asked from Thee, Thou hast given to him -- length of days, Age-during -- and for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Great [is] his honour in Thy salvation, Honour and majesty Thou placest on him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou makest him blessings for ever, Thou dost cause him to rejoice with joy, By Thy countenance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the king is trusting in Jehovah, And in the kindness of the Most High He is not moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy hand cometh to all Thine enemies, Thy right hand doth find Thy haters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou makest them as a furnace of fire, At the time of Thy presence. Jehovah in His anger doth swallow them, And fire doth devour them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their fruit from earth Thou destroyest, And their seed from the sons of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they stretched out against Thee evil, They devised a wicked device, they prevail not,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou makest them a butt, When Thy strings Thou preparest against their faces.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be Thou exalted, O Jehovah in, Thy strength, We sing and we praise Thy might!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, on 'The Hind of the Morning.' -- A Psalm of David. My God, my God, why hast Thou forsaken me? Far from my salvation, The words of my roaring?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My God, I call by day, and Thou answerest not, And by night, and there is no silence to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou [art] holy, Sitting -- the Praise of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Thee did our fathers trust -- they trusted, And Thou dost deliver them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unto Thee they cried, and were delivered, In Thee they trusted, and were not ashamed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I [am] a worm, and no man, A reproach of man, and despised of the people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All beholding me do mock at me, They make free with the lip -- shake the head,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Roll unto Jehovah, He doth deliver him, He doth deliver him, for he delighted in him.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou [art] He bringing me forth from the womb, Causing me to trust, On the breasts of my mother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On Thee I have been cast from the womb, From the belly of my mother Thou [art] my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be not far from me, For adversity is near, for there is no helper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many bulls have surrounded me, Mighty ones of Bashan have compassed me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have opened against me their mouth, A lion tearing and roaring.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As waters I have been poured out, And separated themselves have all my bones, My heart hath been like wax, It is melted in the midst of my bowels.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dried up as an earthen vessel is my power, And my tongue is cleaving to my jaws.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to the dust of death thou appointest me, For surrounded me have dogs, A company of evil doers have compassed me, Piercing my hands and my feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I count all my bones -- they look expectingly, They look upon me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They apportion my garments to themselves, And for my clothing they cause a lot to fall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, O Jehovah, be not far off, O my strength, to help me haste.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Deliver from the sword my soul, From the paw of a dog mine only one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Save me from the mouth of a lion: -- And -- from the horns of the high places Thou hast answered me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I declare Thy name to my brethren, In the midst of the assembly I praise Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ye who fear Jehovah, praise ye Him, All the seed of Jacob, honour ye Him, And be afraid of Him, all ye seed of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He hath not despised, nor abominated, The affliction of the afflicted, Nor hath He hidden His face from him, And in his crying unto Him He heareth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of Thee my praise [is] in the great assembly. My vows I complete before His fearers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The humble do eat and are satisfied, Praise Jehovah do those seeking Him, Your heart doth live for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember and return unto Jehovah, Do all ends of the earth, And before Thee bow themselves, Do all families of the nations,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For to Jehovah [is] the kingdom, And He is ruling among nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the fat ones of earth have eaten, And they bow themselves, Before Him bow do all going down to dust, And he [who] hath not revived his soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A seed doth serve Him, It is declared of the Lord to the generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They come and declare His righteousness, To a people that is borne, that He hath made!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David. Jehovah [is] my shepherd, I do not lack,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In pastures of tender grass He causeth me to lie down, By quiet waters He doth lead me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul He refresheth, He leadeth me in paths of righteousness, For His name's sake,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- when I walk in a valley of death-shade, I fear no evil, for Thou [art] with me, Thy rod and Thy staff -- they comfort me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou arrangest before me a table, Over-against my adversaries, Thou hast anointed with oil my head, My cup is full!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- goodness and kindness pursue me, All the days of my life, And my dwelling [is] in the house of Jehovah, For a length of days!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David. To Jehovah [is] the earth and its fulness, The world and the inhabitants in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He on the seas hath founded it, And on the floods He doth establish it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who goeth up into the hill of Jehovah? And who riseth up in His holy place?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The clean of hands, and pure of heart, Who hath not lifted up to vanity his soul, Nor hath sworn to deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He beareth away a blessing from Jehovah, Righteousness from the God of his salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This [is] a generation of those seeking Him. Seeking Thy face, O Jacob! Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lift up, O gates, your heads, And be lifted up, O doors age-during, And come in doth the king of glory!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] this -- 'the king of glory?' Jehovah -- strong and mighty, Jehovah, the mighty in battle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lift up, O gates, your heads, And be lifted up, O doors age-during, And come in doth the king of glory!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] He -- this 'king of glory?' Jehovah of hosts -- He [is] the king of glory! Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Unto Thee, O Jehovah, my soul I lift up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My God, in Thee I have trusted, Let me not be ashamed, Let not mine enemies exult over me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also let none waiting on Thee be ashamed, Let the treacherous dealers without cause be ashamed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy ways, O Jehovah, cause me to know, Thy paths teach Thou me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cause me to tread in Thy truth, and teach me, For Thou [art] the God of my salvation, Near Thee I have waited all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember Thy mercies, O Jehovah, And Thy kindnesses, for from the age [are] they.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sins of my youth, and my transgressions, Do not Thou remember. According to Thy kindness be mindful of me, For Thy goodness' sake, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Good and upright [is] Jehovah, Therefore He directeth sinners in the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He causeth the humble to tread in judgment, And teacheth the humble His way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the paths of Jehovah [are] kindness and truth, To those keeping His covenant, And His testimonies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thy name's sake, O Jehovah, Thou hast pardoned mine iniquity, for it [is] great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] this -- the man fearing Jehovah? He directeth him in the way He doth choose.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His soul in good doth remain, And his seed doth possess the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The secret of Jehovah [is] for those fearing Him, And His covenant -- to cause them to know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine eyes [are] continually unto Jehovah, For He bringeth out from a net my feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn Thou unto me, and favour me, For lonely and afflicted [am] I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The distresses of my heart have enlarged themselves, From my distresses bring me out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See mine affliction and my misery, And bear with all my sins.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See my enemies, for they have been many, And with violent hatred they have hated me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Keep my soul, and deliver me, Let me not be ashamed, for I trusted in Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Integrity and uprightness do keep me, For I have waited [on] Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Redeem Israel, O God, from all his distresses!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Judge me, O Jehovah, for I in mine integrity have walked, And in Jehovah I have trusted, I slide not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Try me, O Jehovah, and prove me, Purified [are] my reins and my heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thy kindness [is] before mine eyes, And I have walked habitually in Thy truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have not sat with vain men, And with dissemblers I enter not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have hated the assembly of evil doers, And with the wicked I sit not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I wash in innocency my hands, And I compass Thine altar, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To sound with a voice of confession, And to recount all Thy wonders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, I have loved the habitation of Thy house, And the place of the tabernacle of Thine honour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do not gather with sinners my soul, And with men of blood my life,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In whose hand [is] a wicked device, And their right hand [is] full of bribes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, in mine integrity I walk, Redeem me, and favour me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My foot hath stood in uprightness, In assemblies I bless Jehovah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Jehovah [is] my light and my salvation, Whom do I fear? Jehovah [is] the strength of my life, Of whom am I afraid?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When evil doers come near to me to eat my flesh, My adversaries and mine enemies to me, They have stumbled and fallen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though a host doth encamp against me, My heart doth not fear, Though war riseth up against me, In this I [am] confident.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One [thing] I asked of Jehovah -- it I seek. My dwelling in the house of Jehovah, All the days of my life, To look on the pleasantness of Jehovah, And to inquire in His temple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He hideth me in a tabernacle in the day of evil, He hideth me in a secret place of His tent, On a rock he raiseth me up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, lifted up is my head, Above my enemies -- my surrounders, And I sacrifice in His tent sacrifices of shouting, I sing, yea, I sing praise to Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O Jehovah, my voice -- I call, And favour me, and answer me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To Thee said my heart 'They sought my face, Thy face, O Jehovah, I seek.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hide not Thy face from me, Turn not aside in anger Thy servant, My help Thou hast been. Leave me not, nor forsake me, O God of my salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When my father and my mother Have forsaken me, then doth Jehovah gather me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Shew me, O Jehovah, Thy way, And lead me in a path of uprightness, For the sake of my beholders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give me not to the will of my adversaries, For risen against me have false witnesses, And they breathe out violence to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I had not believed to look on the goodness of Jehovah In the land of the living!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look unto Jehovah -- be strong, And He doth strengthen thy heart, Yea, look unto Jehovah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Unto Thee, O Jehovah, I call, My rock, be not silent to me! Lest Thou be silent to me, And I have been compared With those going down to the pit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear the voice of my supplications, In my crying unto Thee, In my lifting up my hands toward thy holy oracle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Draw me not with the wicked, And with workers of iniquity, Speaking peace with their neighbours, And evil in their heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give to them according to their acting, And according to the evil of their doings. According to the work of their hands give to them. Return their deed to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they attend not to the doing of Jehovah, And unto the work of His hands. He throweth them down, And doth not build them up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] Jehovah, For He hath heard the voice of my supplications.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] my strength, and my shield, In Him my heart trusted, and I have been helped. And my heart exulteth, And with my song I thank Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah [is] strength to him, Yea, the strength of the salvation of His anointed [is] He.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Save Thy people, and bless Thine inheritance, And feed them, and carry them to the age!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David. Ascribe to Jehovah, ye sons of the mighty, Ascribe to Jehovah honour and strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ascribe to Jehovah the honour of His name, Bow yourselves to Jehovah, In the beauty of holiness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah [is] on the waters, The God of glory hath thundered, Jehovah [is] on many waters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah [is] with power, The voice of Jehovah [is] with majesty,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah [is] shivering cedars, Yea, Jehovah shivers the cedars of Lebanon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He causeth them to skip as a calf, Lebanon and Sirion as a son of Reems,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah is hewing fiery flames,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah paineth a wilderness, Jehovah paineth the wilderness of Kadesh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Jehovah paineth the oaks, And maketh bare the forests, And in His temple every one saith, 'Glory.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah on the deluge hath sat, And Jehovah sitteth king -- to the age,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah strength to his people giveth, Jehovah blesseth His people with peace!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm. -- A song of the dedication of the house of David. I exalt Thee, O Jehovah, For Thou hast drawn me up, and hast not let mine enemies rejoice over me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah my God, I have cried to Thee, And Thou dost heal me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, Thou hast brought up from Sheol my soul, Thou hast kept me alive, From going down [to] the pit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing praise to Jehovah, ye His saints, And give thanks at the remembrance of His holiness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For -- a moment [is] in His anger, Life [is] in His good-will, At even remaineth weeping, and at morn singing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- I have said in mine ease, 'I am not moved -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, in Thy good pleasure, Thou hast caused strength to remain for my mountain,' Thou hast hidden Thy face -- I have been troubled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unto Thee, O Jehovah, I call, And unto Jehovah I make supplication.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'What gain [is] in my blood? In my going down unto corruption? Doth dust thank Thee? doth it declare Thy truth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O Jehovah, and favour me, O Jehovah, be a helper to me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast turned my mourning to dancing for me, Thou hast loosed my sackcloth, And girdest me [with] joy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that honour doth praise Thee, and is not silent, O Jehovah, my God, to the age I thank Thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. In Thee, O Jehovah, I have trusted, Let me not be ashamed to the age, In Thy righteousness deliver me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Incline unto me Thine ear hastily, deliver me, Be to me for a strong rock, For a house of bulwarks to save me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my rock and my bulwark [art] Thou, For Thy name's sake lead me and tend me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bring me out from the net that they hid for me, For Thou [art] my strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Into Thy hand I commit my spirit, Thou hast redeemed me, Jehovah God of truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have hated the observers of lying vanities, And I toward Jehovah have been confident.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I rejoice, and am glad in Thy kindness, In that Thou hast seen mine affliction, Thou hast known in adversities my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou hast not shut me up, Into the hand of an enemy, Thou hast caused my feet to stand in a broad place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Favour me, O Jehovah, for distress [is] to me, Mine eye, my soul, and my body Have become old by provocation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my life hath been consumed in sorrow And my years in sighing. Feeble because of mine iniquity hath been my strength, And my bones have become old.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Among all mine adversaries I have been a reproach, And to my neighbours exceedingly, And a fear to mine acquaintances, Those seeing me without -- fled from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been forgotten as dead out of mind, I have been as a perishing vessel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have heard an evil account of many, Fear [is] round about. In their being united against me, To take my life they have devised,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I on Thee -- I have trusted, O Jehovah, I have said, 'Thou [art] my God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Thy hand [are] my times, Deliver me from the hand of my enemies, And from my pursuers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cause Thy face to shine on Thy servant, Save me in Thy kindness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, let me not be ashamed, For I have called Thee, let the wicked be ashamed, Let them become silent to Sheol.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let lips of falsehood become dumb, That are speaking against the righteous, Ancient sayings, in pride and contempt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How abundant is Thy goodness, That Thou hast laid up for those fearing Thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast wrought for those trusting in Thee, Before sons of men. Thou hidest them in the secret place of Thy presence, From artifices of man, Thou concealest them in a tabernacle, From the strife of tongues.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] Jehovah, For He hath made marvellous His kindness To me in a city of bulwarks.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- I have said in my haste, 'I have been cut off from before Thine eyes,' But Thou hast heard the voice of my supplications, In my crying unto Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Love Jehovah, all ye His saints, Jehovah is keeping the faithful, And recompensing abundantly a proud doer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be strong, and He strengtheneth your heart, All ye who are waiting for Jehovah!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. -- An Instruction. O the happiness of him whose transgression [is] forgiven, Whose sin is covered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of a man, To whom Jehovah imputeth not iniquity, And in whose spirit there is no deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When I have kept silence, become old have my bones, Through my roaring all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When by day and by night Thy hand is heavy upon me, My moisture hath been changed Into the droughts of summer. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My sin I cause Thee to know, And mine iniquity I have not covered. I have said, 'I confess concerning My transgressions to Jehovah,' And Thou -- Thou hast taken away, The iniquity of my sin. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For this doth every saintly one pray to Thee, As the time to find. Surely at an overflowing of many waters, Unto him they come not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou [art] a hiding-place for me, From distress Thou dost keep me, [With] songs of deliverance dost compass me. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I cause thee to act wisely, And direct thee in the way that thou goest, I cause mine eye to take counsel concerning thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be ye not as a horse -- as a mule, Without understanding, With bridle and bit, its ornaments, to curb, Not to come near unto thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many [are] the pains of the wicked; As to him who is trusting in Jehovah, Kindness doth compass him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be glad in Jehovah, and rejoice, ye righteous, And sing, all ye upright of heart!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing, ye righteous, in Jehovah, For upright ones praise [is] comely.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give ye thanks to Jehovah with a harp, With psaltery of ten strings sing praise to Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing ye to Him a new song, Play skilfully with shouting.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For upright [is] the word of Jehovah, And all His work [is] in faithfulness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Loving righteousness and judgment, Of the kindness of Jehovah is the earth full.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the word of Jehovah The heavens have been made, And by the breath of His mouth all their host.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gathering as a heap the waters of the sea, Putting in treasuries the depths.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Afraid of Jehovah are all the earth, Of Him are all the inhabitants of the world afraid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He hath said, and it is, He hath commanded, and it standeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah made void the counsel of nations, He disallowed the thoughts of the peoples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The counsel of Jehovah to the age standeth, The thoughts of His heart to all generations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of the nation whose God [is] Jehovah, Of the people He did choose, For an inheritance to Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the heavens hath Jehovah looked, He hath seen all the sons of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the fixed place of His dwelling, He looked unto all inhabitants of the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is forming their hearts together, Who is attending unto all their works.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The king is not saved by the multitude of a force. A mighty man is not delivered, By abundance of power.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A false thing [is] the horse for safety, And by the abundance of his strength He doth not deliver.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, the eye of Jehovah [is] to those fearing Him, To those waiting for His kindness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To deliver from death their soul, And to keep them alive in famine.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Our soul hath waited for Jehovah, Our help and our shield [is] He,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For in Him doth our heart rejoice, For in His holy name we have trusted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let Thy kindness, O Jehovah, be upon us, As we have waited for Thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David, in his changing his behaviour before Abimelech, and he driveth him away, and he goeth. I do bless Jehovah at all times, Continually His praise [is] in my mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Jehovah doth my soul boast herself, Hear do the humble and rejoice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ascribe ye greatness to Jehovah with me, And we exalt His name together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I sought Jehovah, and He answered me, And from all my fears did deliver me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They looked expectingly unto Him, And they became bright, And their faces are not ashamed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This poor [one] called, and Jehovah heard, And from all his distresses saved him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A messenger of Jehovah is encamping, Round about those who fear Him, And He armeth them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Taste ye and see that Jehovah [is] good, O the happiness of the man who trusteth in Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fear Jehovah, ye His holy ones, For there is no lack to those fearing Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Young lions have lacked and been hungry, And those seeking Jehovah lack not any good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come ye, children, hearken to me, The fear of Jehovah I do teach you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] the man that is desiring life? Loving days to see good?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Keep thy tongue from evil, And thy lips from speaking deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn aside from evil and do good, Seek peace and pursue it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The eyes of Jehovah [are] unto the righteous, And His ears unto their cry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (The face of Jehovah [is] on doers of evil, To cut off from earth their memorial.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They cried, and Jehovah heard, And from all their distresses delivered them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Near [is] Jehovah to the broken of heart, And the bruised of spirit He saveth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Many [are] the evils of the righteous, Out of them all doth Jehovah deliver him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is keeping all his bones, One of them hath not been broken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Evil doth put to death the wicked, And those hating the righteous are desolate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah redeemeth the soul of His servants, And none trusting in Him are desolate!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Strive, Jehovah, with my strivers, fight with my fighters,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Take hold of shield and buckler, and rise for my help,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And draw out spear and lance, To meet my pursuers. Say to my soul, 'Thy salvation I [am].'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are ashamed and blush, those seeking my soul, Turned backward and confounded, Those devising my evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are as chaff before wind, And a messenger of Jehovah driving away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their way is darkness and slipperiness, And a messenger of Jehovah their pursuer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For without cause they hid for me their netpit, Without cause they digged for my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Meet him doth desolation -- he knoweth not, And his net that he hid catcheth him, For desolation he falleth into it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my soul is joyful in Jehovah, It rejoiceth in His salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All my bones say, 'Jehovah, who is like Thee, Delivering the poor from the stronger than he, And the poor and needy from his plunderer.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Violent witnesses rise up, That which I have not known they ask me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They pay me evil for good, bereaving my soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- in their sickness my clothing [is] sackcloth, I have humbled with fastings my soul, And my prayer unto my bosom returneth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As [if] a friend, as [if] my brother, I walked habitually, As a mourner for a mother, Mourning I have bowed down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And -- in my halting they have rejoiced, And have been gathered together, Gathered against me were the smiters, And I have not known, They have rent, and they have not ceased;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With profane ones, mockers in feasts, Gnashing against me their teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lord, how long dost thou behold? Keep back my soul from their desolations, From young lions my only one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I thank Thee in a great assembly, Among a mighty people I praise Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
