translations.text: Mine enemies rejoice not over me [with] falsehood, Those hating me without cause wink the eye.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they speak not peace, And against the quiet of the land, Deceitful words they devise,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they enlarge against me their mouth, They said, 'Aha, aha, our eye hath seen.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast seen, O Jehovah, Be not silent, O Lord -- be not far from me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stir up, and wake to my judgment, My God, and my Lord, to my plea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judge me according to Thy righteousness, O Jehovah my God, And they do not rejoice over me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They do not say in their heart, 'Aha, our desire.' They do not say, 'We swallowed him up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are ashamed and confounded together, Who are rejoicing at my evil. They put on shame and confusion, Who are magnifying themselves against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They sing and rejoice, who are desiring my righteousness, And they say continually, 'Jehovah is magnified, Who is desiring the peace of His servant.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my tongue uttereth Thy righteousness, All the day Thy praise!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By a servant of Jehovah, by David. The transgression of the wicked Is affirming within my heart, 'Fear of God is not before his eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he made [it] smooth to himself in his eyes, To find his iniquity to be hated.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The words of his mouth [are] iniquity and deceit, He ceased to act prudently -- to do good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Iniquity he deviseth on his bed, He stationeth himself on a way not good, Evil he doth not refuse.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, in the heavens [is] Thy kindness, Thy faithfulness [is] unto the clouds.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy righteousness [is] as mountains of God, Thy judgments [are] a great deep. Man and beast Thou savest, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How precious [is] Thy kindness, O God, And the sons of men In the shadow of Thy wings do trust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are filled from the fatness of Thy house, And the stream of Thy delights Thou dost cause them to drink.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For with Thee [is] a fountain of life, In Thy light we see light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Draw out Thy kindness to those knowing Thee, And Thy righteousness to the upright of heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not a foot of pride meet me, And a hand of the wicked let not move me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There have workers of iniquity fallen, They have been overthrown, And have not been able to arise!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By David. Do not fret because of evil doers, Be not envious against doers of iniquity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For as grass speedily they are cut off, And as the greenness of the tender grass do fade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Trust in Jehovah, and do good, Dwell [in] the land, and enjoy faithfulness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And delight thyself on Jehovah, And He giveth to thee the petitions of thy heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Roll on Jehovah thy way, And trust upon Him, and He worketh,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And hath brought out as light thy righteousness, And thy judgment as noon-day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be silent for Jehovah, and stay thyself for Him, Do not fret because of him Who is making prosperous his way, Because of a man doing wicked devices.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Desist from anger, and forsake fury, Fret not thyself only to do evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For evil doers are cut off, As to those waiting on Jehovah, they possess the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet a little, and the wicked is not, And thou hast considered his place, and it is not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the humble do possess the land, And they have delighted themselves In the abundance of peace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked is devising against the righteous, And gnashing against him his teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Lord doth laugh at him, For He hath seen that his day cometh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A sword have the wicked opened, And they have trodden their bow, To cause to fall the poor and needy, To slaughter the upright of the way.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their sword doth enter into their own heart, And their bows are shivered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Better [is] the little of the righteous, Than the store of many wicked.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the arms of the wicked are shivered, And Jehovah is sustaining the righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah knoweth the days of the perfect, And their inheritance is -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are not ashamed in a time of evil, And in days of famine they are satisfied.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But the wicked perish, and the enemies of Jehovah, As the preciousness of lambs, Have been consumed, In smoke they have been consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked is borrowing and repayeth not, And the righteous is gracious and giving.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For His blessed ones do possess the land, And His reviled ones are cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From Jehovah [are] the steps of a man, They have been prepared, And his way he desireth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When he falleth, he is not cast down, For Jehovah is sustaining his hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Young I have been, I have also become old, And I have not seen the righteous forsaken, And his seed seeking bread.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the day he is gracious and lending, And his seed [is] for a blessing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn aside from evil, and do good, and dwell to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah is loving judgment, And He doth not forsake His saintly ones, To the age they have been kept, And the seed of the wicked is cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The righteous possess the land, And they dwell for ever on it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The mouth of the righteous uttereth wisdom, And his tongue speaketh judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The law of his God [is] his heart, His steps do not slide.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked is watching for the righteous, And is seeking to put him to death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth not leave him in his hand, Nor condemn him in his being judged.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look unto Jehovah, and keep His way, And He doth exalt thee to possess the land, In the wicked being cut off -- thou seest!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have seen the wicked terrible, And spreading as a green native plant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he passeth away, and lo, he is not, And I seek him, and he is not found!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Observe the perfect, and see the upright, For the latter end of each [is] peace.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And transgressors were destroyed together, The latter end of the wicked was cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the salvation of the righteous [is] from Jehovah, Their strong place in a time of adversity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah doth help them and deliver them, He delivereth them from the wicked, And saveth them, Because they trusted in Him!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David, 'To cause to remember.' Jehovah, in Thy wrath reprove me not, Nor in Thy fury chastise me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thine arrows have come down on me, And Thou lettest down upon me Thy hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Soundness is not in my flesh, Because of Thine indignation, Peace is not in my bones because of my sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For mine iniquities have passed over my head, As a heavy burden -- too heavy for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stunk -- become corrupt have my wounds, Because of my folly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been bent down, I have been bowed down -- unto excess, All the day I have gone mourning.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my flanks have been full of drought, And soundness is not in my flesh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been feeble and smitten -- unto excess, I have roared from disquietude of heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lord, before Thee [is] all my desire, And my sighing from Thee hath not been hid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My heart [is] panting, my power hath forsaken me, And the light of mine eyes, Even they are not with me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My lovers and my friends over-against my plague stand. And my neighbours afar off have stood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those seeking my soul lay a snare, And those seeking my evil Have spoken mischievous things, And they do deceits meditate all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, as deaf, hear not. And as a dumb one who openeth not his mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, I am as a man who heareth not, And in his mouth are no reproofs.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because for Thee, O Jehovah, I have waited, Thou dost answer, O Lord my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When I said, 'Lest they rejoice over me, In the slipping of my foot against me they magnified themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I am ready to halt, And my pain [is] before me continually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For mine iniquity I declare, I am sorry for my sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And mine enemies [are] lively, They have been strong, and those hating me without cause, Have been multiplied.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And those paying evil for good accuse me, Because of my pursuing good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do not forsake me, O Jehovah, My God, be not far from me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Haste to help me, O Lord, my salvation!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, to Jeduthun. -- A Psalm of David. I have said, 'I observe my ways, Against sinning with my tongue, I keep for my mouth a curb, while the wicked [is] before me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I was dumb [with] silence, I kept silent from good, and my pain is excited.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hot [is] my heart within me, In my meditating doth the fire burn, I have spoken with my tongue.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Cause me to know, O Jehovah, mine end, And the measure of my days -- what it [is],' I know how frail I [am].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, handbreadths Thou hast made my days, And mine age [is] as nothing before Thee, Only, all vanity [is] every man set up. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, in an image doth each walk habitually, Only, [in] vain, they are disquieted, He heapeth up and knoweth not who gathereth them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, now, what have I expected? O Lord, my hope -- it [is] of Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From all my transgressions deliver me, A reproach of the fool make me not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been dumb, I open not my mouth, Because Thou -- Thou hast done [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn aside from off me Thy stroke, From the striving of Thy hand I have been consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With reproofs against iniquity, Thou hast corrected man, And dost waste as a moth his desirableness, Only, vanity [is] every man. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear my prayer, O Jehovah, And [to] my cry give ear, Unto my tear be not silent, For a sojourner I [am] with Thee, A settler like all my fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look from me, and I brighten up before I go and am not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. I have diligently expected Jehovah, And He inclineth to me, and heareth my cry,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He doth cause me to come up From a pit of desolation -- from mire of mud, And He raiseth up on a rock my feet, He is establishing my steps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He putteth in my mouth a new song, 'Praise to our God.' Many do see and fear, and trust in Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of the man Who hath made Jehovah his trust, And hath not turned unto the proud, And those turning aside to lies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Much hast Thou done, Jehovah my God; Thy wonders and Thy thoughts toward us, There is none to arrange unto Thee, I declare and speak: They have been more than to be numbered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sacrifice and present Thou hast not desired, Ears Thou hast prepared for me, Burnt and sin-offering Thou hast not asked.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then said I, 'Lo, I have come,' In the roll of the book it is written of me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To do Thy pleasure, my God, I have delighted, And Thy law [is] within my heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have proclaimed tidings of righteousness In the great assembly, lo, my lips I restrain not, O Jehovah, Thou hast known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy righteousness I have not concealed In the midst of my heart, Thy faithfulness and Thy salvation I have told, I have not hidden Thy kindness and Thy truth, To the great assembly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, O Jehovah, restrainest not Thy mercies from me, Thy kindness and Thy truth do continually keep me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For compassed me have evils innumerable, Overtaken me have mine iniquities, And I have not been able to see; They have been more than the hairs of my head, And my heart hath forsaken me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be pleased, O Jehovah, to deliver me, O Jehovah, for my help make haste.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are ashamed and confounded together, Who are seeking my soul to destroy it, They are turned backward, And are ashamed, who are desiring my evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are desolate because of their shame, Who are saying to me, 'Aha, aha.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All seeking Thee rejoice and are glad in Thee, Those loving Thy salvation say continually, 'Jehovah is magnified.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I [am] poor and needy, The Lord doth devise for me. My help and my deliverer [art] Thou, O my God, tarry Thou not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. O the happiness of him Who is acting wisely unto the poor, In a day of evil doth Jehovah deliver him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth preserve him and revive him, He is happy in the land, And Thou givest him not into the will of his enemies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah supporteth on a couch of sickness, All his bed Thou hast turned in his weakness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I said, 'O Jehovah, favour me, Heal my soul, for I did sin against Thee,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine enemies say evil of me: When he dieth -- his name hath perished!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if he came to see -- vanity he speaketh, His heart gathereth iniquity to itself, He goeth out -- at the street he speaketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All hating me whisper together against me, Against me they devise evil to me:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A thing of Belial is poured out on him, And because he lay down he riseth not again.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Even mine ally, in whom I trusted, One eating my bread, made great the heel against me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, Jehovah, favour me, And cause me to rise, And I give recompence to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By this I have known, That Thou hast delighted in me, Because my enemy shouteth not over me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to me, in mine integrity, Thou hast taken hold upon me, And causest me to stand before Thee to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] Jehovah, God of Israel, From the age -- and unto the age. Amen and Amen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- An Instruction. By sons of Korah. As a hart doth pant for streams of water, So my soul panteth toward Thee, O God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul thirsted for God, for the living God, When do I enter and see the face of God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My tear hath been to me bread day and night, In their saying unto me all the day, 'Where [is] thy God?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These I remember, and pour out my soul in me, For I pass over into the booth, I go softly with them unto the house of God, With the voice of singing and confession, The multitude keeping feast!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What! bowest thou thyself, O my soul? Yea, art thou troubled within me? Wait for God, for still I confess Him: The salvation of my countenance -- My God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In me doth my soul bow itself, Therefore I remember Thee from the land of Jordan, And of the Hermons, from the hill Mizar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Deep unto deep is calling At the noise of Thy water-spouts, All Thy breakers and Thy billows passed over me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By day Jehovah commandeth His kindness, And by night a song [is] with me, A prayer to the God of my life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say to God my rock, 'Why hast Thou forgotten me? Why go I mourning in the oppression of an enemy?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With a sword in my bones Have mine adversaries reproached me, In their saying unto me all the day, 'Where [is] thy God?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What! bowest thou thyself, O my soul? And what! art thou troubled within me? Wait for God, for still I confess Him, The salvation of my countenance, and my God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judge me, O God, And plead my cause against a nation not pious, From a man of deceit and perverseness Thou dost deliver me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou [art] the God of my strength. Why hast Thou cast me off? Why mourning do I go up and down, In the oppression of an enemy?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Send forth Thy light and Thy truth, They -- they lead me, they bring me in, Unto Thy holy hill, and unto Thy tabernacles.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I go in unto the altar of God, Unto God, the joy of my rejoicing. And I thank Thee with a harp, O God, my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What! bowest thou thyself, O my soul? And what! art thou troubled within me? Wait for God, for still I confess Him, The salvation of my countenance, and my God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By sons of Korah. An Instruction. O God, with our ears we have heard, Our fathers have recounted to us, The work Thou didst work in their days, In the days of old.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, [with] Thy hand, nations hast dispossessed. And Thou dost plant them. Thou afflictest peoples, and sendest them away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, not by their sword Possessed they the land, And their arm gave not salvation to them, But Thy right hand, and Thine arm, And the light of Thy countenance, Because Thou hadst accepted them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou [art] He, my king, O God, Command the deliverances of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By Thee our adversaries we do push, By Thy name tread down our withstanders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, not in my bow do I trust, And my sword doth not save me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast saved us from our adversaries, And those hating us Thou hast put to shame.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In God we have boasted all the day, And Thy name to the age we thank. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In anger Thou hast cast off and causest us to blush, And goest not forth with our hosts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou causest us to turn backward from an adversary, And those hating us, Have spoiled for themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou makest us food like sheep, And among nations Thou hast scattered us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou sellest Thy people -- without wealth, And hast not become great by their price.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbours, A scorn and a reproach to our surrounders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou makest us a simile among nations, A shaking of the head among peoples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the day my confusion [is] before me, And the shame of my face hath covered me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of the voice of a reproacher and reviler, Because of an enemy and a self-avenger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All this met us, and we did not forget Thee, Nor have we dealt falsely in Thy covenant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We turn not backward our heart, Nor turn aside doth our step from Thy path.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But Thou hast smitten us in a place of dragons, And dost cover us over with death-shade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If we have forgotten the name of our God, And spread our hands to a strange God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth not God search out this? For He knoweth the secrets of the heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely, for Thy sake we have been slain all the day, Reckoned as sheep of the slaughter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stir up -- why dost Thou sleep, O Lord? Awake, cast us not off for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why Thy face hidest Thou? Thou forgettest our afflictions and our oppression,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For bowed to the dust hath our soul, Cleaved to the earth hath our belly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Arise, a help to us, And ransom us for thy kindness' sake.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Lilies.' -- By sons of Korah. -- An Instruction. -- A song of loves. My heart hath indited a good thing, I am telling my works to a king, My tongue [is] the pen of a speedy writer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast been beautified above the sons of men, Grace hath been poured into thy lips, Therefore hath God blessed thee to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gird Thy sword upon the thigh, O mighty, Thy glory and Thy majesty!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to Thy majesty -- prosper! -- ride! Because of truth, and meekness -- righteousness, And Thy right hand showeth Thee fearful things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thine arrows [are] sharp, -- Peoples fall under Thee -- In the heart of the enemies of the king.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy throne, O God, [is] age-during, and for ever, A sceptre of uprightness [Is] the sceptre of Thy kingdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast loved righteousness and hatest wickedness, Therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee, Oil of joy above thy companions.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Myrrh and aloes, cassia! all thy garments, Out of palaces of ivory Stringed instruments have made thee glad.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Daughters of kings [are] among thy precious ones, A queen hath stood at thy right hand, In pure gold of Ophir.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hearken, O daughter, and see, incline thine ear, And forget thy people, and thy father's house,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the king doth desire thy beauty, Because he [is] thy lord -- bow thyself to him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the daughter of Tyre with a present, The rich of the people do appease thy face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All glory [is] the daughter of the king within, Of gold-embroidered work [is] her clothing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In divers colours she is brought to the king, Virgins -- after her -- her companions, Are brought to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are brought with joy and gladness, They come into the palace of the king.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Instead of thy fathers are thy sons, Thou dost appoint them for princes in all the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I make mention of Thy name in all generations, Therefore do peoples praise Thee, To the age, and for ever!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By sons of Korah. 'For the Virgins.' -- A song. God [is] to us a refuge and strength, A help in adversities found most surely.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore we fear not in the changing of earth, And in the slipping of mountains Into the heart of the seas.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Roar -- troubled are its waters, Mountains they shake in its pride. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A river -- its rivulets rejoice the city of God, Thy holy place of the tabernacles of the Most High.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God [is] in her midst -- she is not moved, God doth help her at the turn of the morn!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Troubled have been nations, Moved have been kingdoms, He hath given forth with His voice, earth melteth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah of Hosts [is] with us, A tower for us [is] the God of Jacob. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come ye, see the works of Jehovah, Who hath done astonishing things in the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Causing wars to cease, Unto the end of the earth, the bow he shivereth, And the spear He hath cut asunder, Chariots he doth burn with fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Desist, and know that I [am] God, I am exalted among nations, I am exalted in the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah of hosts [is] with us, A tower for us [is] the God of Jacob! Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By sons of Korah. A Psalm. All ye peoples, clap the hand, Shout to God with a voice of singing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah Most High [is] fearful, A great king over all the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He leadeth peoples under us, and nations under our feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth choose for us our inheritance, The excellency of Jacob that He loves. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath gone up with a shout, Jehovah with the sound of a trumpet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Praise God -- praise -- give praise to our king, praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For king of all the earth [is] God, Give praise, O understanding one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath reigned over nations, God hath sat on His holy throne,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Nobles of peoples have been gathered, [With] the people of the God of Abraham, For to God [are] the shields of earth, Greatly hath He been exalted!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Song, a Psalm, by sons of Korah. Great [is] Jehovah, and praised greatly, In the city of our God -- His holy hill.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Beautiful [for] elevation, A joy of all the land, [is] Mount Zion, The sides of the north, the city of a great king.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God in her high places is known for a tower.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, lo, the kings met, they passed by together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have seen -- so they have marvelled, They have been troubled, they were hastened away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Trembling hath seized them there, Pain, as of a travailing woman.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By an east wind Thou shiverest ships of Tarshish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As we have heard, so we have seen, In the city of Jehovah of hosts, In the city of our God, God doth establish her -- to the age. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We have thought, O God, of Thy kindness, In the midst of Thy temple,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As [is] Thy name, O God, so [is] Thy praise, Over the ends of the earth, Righteousness hath filled Thy right hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rejoice doth Mount Zion, The daughters of Judah are joyful, For the sake of Thy judgments.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Compass Zion, and go round her, count her towers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Set your heart to her bulwark, Consider her high places, So that ye recount to a later generation,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That this God [is] our God -- To the age and for ever, He -- he doth lead us over death!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By sons of Korah. A Psalm. Hear this, all ye peoples, Give ear, all ye inhabitants of the world.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Both low and high, together rich and needy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My mouth speaketh wise things, And the meditations of my heart [are] things of understanding.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I incline to a simile mine ear, I open with a harp my riddle:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why do I fear in days of evil? The iniquity of my supplanters doth compass me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those trusting on their wealth, And in the multitude of their riches, Do shew themselves foolish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A brother doth no one at all ransom, He doth not give to God his atonement.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And precious [is] the redemption of their soul, And it hath ceased -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And still he liveth for ever, He seeth not the pit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he seeth wise men die, Together the foolish and brutish perish, And have left to others their wealth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their heart [is]: Their houses [are] to the age, Their tabernacles to all generations. They proclaimed their names over the lands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And man in honour doth not remain, He hath been like the beasts, they have been cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This their way [is] folly for them, And their posterity with their sayings are pleased. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As sheep for Sheol they have set themselves, Death doth afflict them, And the upright rule over them in the morning, And their form [is] for consumption. Sheol [is] a dwelling for him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, God doth ransom my soul from the hand of Sheol, For He doth receive me. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fear not, when one maketh wealth, When the honour of his house is abundant,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For at his death he receiveth nothing, His honour goeth not down after him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For his soul in his life he blesseth, (And they praise thee when thou dost well for thyself.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It cometh to the generation of his fathers, For ever they see not the light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Man in honour, who understandest not, Hath been like the beasts, they have been cut off!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of Asaph. The God of gods -- Jehovah -- hath spoken, And He calleth to the earth From the rising of the sun unto its going in.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From Zion, the perfection of beauty, God shone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Our God cometh, and is not silent, Fire before Him doth devour, And round about him it hath been very tempestuous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth call unto the heavens from above, And unto the earth, to judge His people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gather ye to Me My saints, Making covenant with Me over a sacrifice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the heavens declare His righteousness, For God Himself [is] judge. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O My people, and I speak, O Israel, and I testify against thee, God, thy God [am] I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not for thy sacrifices do I reprove thee, Yea, thy burnt-offerings [Are] before Me continually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I take not from thy house a bullock, From thy folds he goats.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Mine [is] every beast of the forest, The cattle on the hills of oxen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have known every fowl of the mountains, And the wild beast of the field [is] with Me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I am hungry I tell not to thee, For Mine [is] the world and its fulness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do I eat the flesh of bulls, And drink the blood of he-goats?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sacrifice to God confession, And complete to the Most High thy vows.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And call Me in a day of adversity, I deliver thee, and thou honourest Me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And to the wicked hath God said: What to thee -- to recount My statutes? That thou liftest up My covenant on thy mouth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, thou hast hated instruction, And dost cast My words behind thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou hast seen a thief, Then thou art pleased with him, And with adulterers [is] thy portion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy mouth thou hast sent forth with evil, And thy tongue joineth deceit together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou sittest, against thy brother thou speakest, Against a son of thy mother givest slander.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These thou didst, and I kept silent, Thou hast thought that I am like thee, I reprove thee, and set in array before thine eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Understand this, I pray you, Ye who are forgetting God, Lest I tear, and there is no deliverer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is sacrificing praise honoureth Me, As to him who maketh a way, I cause him to look on the salvation of God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David, in the coming in unto him of Nathan the prophet, when he hath gone in unto Bath-Sheba. Favour me, O God, according to Thy kindness, According to the abundance of Thy mercies, Blot out my transgressions.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thoroughly wash me from mine iniquity, And from my sin cleanse me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my transgressions I do know, And my sin [is] before me continually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Against Thee, Thee only, I have sinned, And done the evil thing in Thine eyes, So that Thou art righteous in Thy words, Thou art pure in Thy judging.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, in iniquity I have been brought forth, And in sin doth my mother conceive me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, truth Thou hast desired in the inward parts, And in the hidden part Wisdom Thou causest me to know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou cleansest me with hyssop and I am clean, Washest me, and than snow I am whiter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou causest me to hear joy and gladness, Thou makest joyful bones Thou hast bruised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hide Thy face from my sin. And all mine iniquities blot out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A clean heart prepare for me, O God, And a right spirit renew within me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cast me not forth from Thy presence, And Thy Holy Spirit take not from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Restore to me the joy of Thy salvation, And a willing spirit doth sustain me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I teach transgressors Thy ways, And sinners unto Thee do return.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Deliver me from blood, O God, God of my salvation, My tongue singeth of Thy righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Lord, my lips thou dost open, And my mouth declareth Thy praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou desirest not sacrifice, or I give [it], Burnt-offering Thou acceptest not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The sacrifices of God [are] a broken spirit, A heart broken and bruised, O God, Thou dost not despise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do good in Thy good pleasure with Zion, Thou dost build the walls of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Thou desirest sacrifices of righteousness, Burnt-offering, and whole burnt-offering, Then they offer bullocks on thine altar!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- An instruction, by David, in the coming in of Doeg the Edomite, and he declareth to Saul, and saith to him, 'David came in unto the house of Ahimelech.' What, boasteth thou in evil, O mighty one? The kindness of God [is] all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mischiefs doth thy tongue devise, Like a sharp razor, working deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast loved evil rather than good, Lying, than speaking righteousness. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast loved all devouring words, O thou deceitful tongue.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- God doth break thee down for ever, Taketh thee, and pulleth thee out of the tent, And He hath uprooted thee Out of the land of the living. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the righteous see, And fear, and laugh at him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Lo, the man who maketh not God his strong place, And trusteth in the abundance of his riches, He is strong in his mischiefs.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, as a green olive in the house of God, I have trusted in the kindness of God, To the age and for ever,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I thank Thee to the age, because Thou hast done [it], And I wait [on] Thy name for [it is] good before Thy saints!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On a disease.' -- An instruction, by David. A fool said in his heart, 'There is no God.' They have done corruptly, Yea, they have done abominable iniquity, There is none doing good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God from the heavens looked on the sons of men, To see if there be an understanding one, [One] seeking God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every one went back, together they became filthy, There is none doing good -- not even one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Have not workers of iniquity known, Those eating my people have eaten bread, God they have not called.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There they feared a fear -- there was no fear, For God hath scattered the bones of him Who is encamping against thee, Thou hast put to shame, For God hath despised them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth give from Zion the salvation of Israel? When God turneth back [to] a captivity of His people, Jacob doth rejoice -- Israel is glad!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer with stringed instruments. -- An instruction, by David, in the coming in of the Ziphim, and they say to Saul, 'Is not David hiding himself with us?' O God, by Thy name save me, and by Thy might judge me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, hear my prayer, Give ear to the sayings of my mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For strangers have risen up against me And terrible ones have sought my soul, They have not set God before them. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, God [is] a helper to me, The Lord [is] with those supporting my soul,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn back doth the evil thing to mine enemies, In Thy truth cut them off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With a free will-offering I sacrifice to Thee, I thank Thy name, O Jehovah, for [it is] good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, from all adversity He delivered me, And on mine enemies hath mine eye looked!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer with stringed instruments. -- An instruction, by David. Give ear, O God, [to] my prayer, And hide not from my supplication.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Attend to me, and answer me, I mourn in my meditation, and make a noise,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of the voice of an enemy, Because of the oppression of the wicked, For they cause sorrow to move against me, And in anger they hate me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My heart is pained within me, And terrors of death have fallen on me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fear and trembling come in to me, And horror doth cover me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I say, 'Who doth give to me a pinion as a dove? I fly away and rest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I move far off, I lodge in a wilderness. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I hasten escape for myself, From a rushing wind, from a whirlwind.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Swallow up, O Lord, divide their tongue, For I saw violence and strife in a city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By day and by night they go round it, on its walls. Both iniquity and perverseness [are] in its midst,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mischiefs [are] in its midst. Fraud and deceit depart not from its street.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For an enemy reproacheth me not, or I bear [it], He who is hating me Hath not magnified himself against me, Or I hide from him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But thou, a man -- as mine equal, My familiar friend, and mine acquaintance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When together we sweeten counsel, Into the house of God we walk in company.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Desolations [are] upon them, They go down [to] Sheol -- alive, For wickedness [is] in their dwelling, in their midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- to God I call, and Jehovah saveth me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Evening, and morning, and noon, I meditate, and make a noise, and He heareth my voice,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath ransomed in peace my soul From him who is near to me, For with the multitude they were with me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God doth hear and afflict them, And He sitteth of old. Selah. Because they have no changes, and fear not God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath sent forth his hands against his well-wishers, He hath polluted his covenant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sweeter than honey hath been his mouth, And his heart [is] war! Softer have been his words than oil, And they [are] drawn [swords].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cast on Jehovah that which He hath given thee, And He doth sustain thee, He doth not suffer for ever the moving of the righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, O God, dost bring them down To a pit of destruction, Men of blood and deceit reach not to half their days, And I -- I do trust in Thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Dumb Dove far off.' -- A secret treasure of David, in the Philistines' taking hold of him in Gath. Favour me, O God, for man swallowed me up, All the day fighting he oppresseth me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine enemies have swallowed up all the day, For many [are] fighting against me, O most High,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The day I am afraid I am confident toward Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In God I praise His word, in God I have trusted, I fear not what flesh doth to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the day they wrest my words, Concerning me all their thoughts [are] for evil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They assemble, they hide, they watch my heels, When they have expected my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By iniquity they escape, In anger the peoples put down, O God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My wandering Thou hast counted, Thou -- place Thou my tear in Thy bottle, Are they not in Thy book?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then turn back do mine enemies in the day I call. This I have known, that God [is] for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In God I praise the word, In Jehovah I praise the word.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In God I trusted, I fear not what man doth to me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On me, O God, [are] Thy vows, I repay thank-offerings to Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast delivered my soul from death, Dost Thou not my feet from falling? To walk habitually before God in the light of the living!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'Destroy not.' -- A secret treasure of David, in his fleeing from the face of Saul into a cave. Favour me, O God, favour me, For in Thee is my soul trusting, And in the shadow of Thy wings I trust, Until the calamities pass over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I call to God Most High, To God [who] is perfecting for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He sendeth from the heaven, and saveth me, He reproached -- who is panting after me. Selah. God sendeth forth His kindness and His truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul [is] in the midst of lions, I lie down [among] flames -- sons of men, Their teeth [are] a spear and arrows, And their tongue a sharp sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be Thou exalted above the heavens, O God, Above all the earth Thine honour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A net they have prepared for my steps, Bowed down hath my soul, They have digged before me a pit, They have fallen into its midst. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Prepared is my heart, O God, Prepared is my heart, I sing and praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Awake, mine honour, awake, psaltery and harp, I awake the morning dawn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I thank Thee among the peoples, O Lord, I praise Thee among the nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For great unto the heavens [is] Thy kindness, And unto the clouds Thy truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be thou exalted above the heavens, O God. Above all the earth Thine honour!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'Destroy not.' -- A secret treasure, by David. Is it true, O dumb one, righteously ye speak? Uprightly ye judge, O sons of men?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Even in heart ye work iniquities, In the land the violence of your hands ye ponder.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wicked have been estranged from the womb, They have erred from the belly, speaking lies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their poison [is] as poison of a serpent, As a deaf asp shutting its ear,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Which hearkeneth not to the voice of whisperers, A charmer of charms most skilful.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, break their teeth in their mouth, The jaw-teeth of young lions break down, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are melted as waters, They go up and down for themselves, His arrow proceedeth as they cut themselves off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a snail that melteth he goeth on, [As] an untimely birth of a woman, They have not seen the sun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before your pots discern the bramble, As well the raw as the heated He whirleth away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The righteous rejoiceth that he hath seen vengeance, His steps he washeth in the blood of the wicked.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And man saith: 'Surely fruit [is] for the righteous: Surely there is a God judging in the earth!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'Destroy not,' by David. -- A secret treasure, in Saul's sending, and they watch the house to put him to death. Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God, From my withstanders set me on high.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Deliver me from workers of iniquity, And from men of blood save me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, lo, they laid wait for my soul, Assembled against me are strong ones, Not my transgression nor my sin, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Without punishment they run and prepare themselves, Stir up to meet me, and see.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, Jehovah, God of Hosts, God of Israel, Awake to inspect all the nations. Favour not any treacherous dealers of iniquity. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They turn back at evening, They make a noise like a dog, And go round about the city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, they belch out with their mouths, Swords [are] in their lips, for 'Who heareth?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, O Jehovah dost laugh at them, Thou dost mock at all the nations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O my Strength, unto Thee I take heed, For God [is] my tower -- the God of my kindness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God doth go before me, He causeth me to look on mine enemies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Slay them not, lest my people forget, Shake them by Thy strength, And bring them down, O Lord our shield.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The sin of their mouth [is] a word of their lips, And they are captured in their pride, And from the curse and lying they recount.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Consume in fury, consume and they are not, And they know that God is ruling in Jacob, To the ends of the earth. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they turn back at evening, They make a noise like a dog, And they go round about the city.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They -- they wander for food, If they are not satisfied -- then they murmur.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- I sing [of] Thy strength, And I sing at morn [of] Thy kindness, For thou hast been a tower to me, And a refuge for me in a day of adversity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O my Strength, unto Thee I sing praise, For God [is] my tower, the God of my kindness!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'Concerning the Lily of Testimony,' a secret treasure of David, to teach, in his striving with Aram-Naharaim, and with Aram-Zobah, and Joab turneth back and smiteth Edom in the valley of Salt -- twelve thousand. O God, Thou hadst cast us off, Thou hadst broken us -- hadst been angry! -- Thou dost turn back to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast caused the land to tremble, Thou hast broken it, Heal its breaches, for it hath moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast shewn Thy people a hard thing, Thou hast caused us to drink wine of trembling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast given to those fearing thee an ensign. To be lifted up as an ensign Because of truth. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That Thy beloved ones may be drawn out, Save [with] Thy right hand, and answer us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath spoken in His holiness: I exult -- I apportion Shechem, And the valley of Succoth I measure,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine [is] Gilead, and mine [is] Manasseh, And Ephraim [is] the strength of my head, Judah [is] my lawgiver,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Moab [is] my pot for washing, over Edom I cast my shoe, Shout, concerning me, O Philistia.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth bring me [to] a city of bulwarks? Who hath led me unto Edom?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it not Thou, O God? hast Thou cast us off? And dost Thou not go forth, O God, with our hosts!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give to us help from adversity, And vain [is] the deliverance of man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In God we do mightily, And He treadeth down our adversaries!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, on stringed instruments. -- By David. Hear, O God, my loud cry, attend to my prayer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the end of the land unto Thee I call, In the feebleness of my heart, Into a rock higher than I Thou dost lead me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast been a refuge for me, A tower of strength because of the enemy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I sojourn in Thy tent to the ages, I trust in the secret place of Thy wings. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou, O God, hast hearkened to my vows, Thou hast appointed the inheritance Of those fearing Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Days to the days of the king Thou addest, His years as generation and generation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He dwelleth to the age before God, Kindness and truth appoint -- they keep him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So do I praise Thy name for ever, When I pay my vows day by day!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, for Jeduthun. -- A Psalm of David. Only -- toward God [is] my soul silent, From Him [is] my salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- He [is] my rock, and my salvation, My tower, I am not much moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when do ye devise mischief against a man? Ye are destroyed all of you, As a wall inclined, a hedge that is cast down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- from his excellency They have consulted to drive away, They enjoy a lie, with their mouth they bless, And with their heart revile. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- for God, be silent, O my soul, For from Him [is] my hope.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- He [is] my rock and my salvation, My tower, I am not moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On God [is] my salvation, and my honour, The rock of my strength, my refuge [is] in God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Trust in Him at all times, O people, Pour forth before Him your heart, God [is] a refuge for us. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- vanity [are] the low, a lie the high. In balances to go up they than vanity [are] lighter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Trust not in oppression, And in robbery become not vain, Wealth -- when it increaseth -- set not the heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Once hath God spoken, twice I heard this, That 'strength [is] with God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And with Thee, O Lord, [is] kindness, For Thou dost recompense to each, According to his work!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of David, in his being in the wilderness of Judah. O God, Thou [art] my God, earnestly do I seek Thee, Thirsted for Thee hath my soul, Longed for Thee hath my flesh, In a land dry and weary, without waters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So in the sanctuary I have seen Thee, To behold Thy strength and Thine honour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because better [is] Thy kindness than life, My lips do praise Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So I bless Thee in my life, in Thy name I lift up my hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As [with] milk and fatness is my soul satisfied, And [with] singing lips doth my mouth praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have remembered Thee on my couch, In the watches -- I meditate on Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast been a help to me, And in the shadow of Thy wings I sing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cleaved hath my soul after Thee, On me hath Thy right hand taken hold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they who for desolation seek my soul, Go in to the lower parts of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They cause him to run on the edge of the sword, A portion for foxes they are.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the king doth rejoice in God, Boast himself doth every one swearing by Him, But stopped is the mouth of those speaking lies!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. Hear, O God, my voice, in my meditation, From the fear of an enemy Thou keepest my life,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hidest me from the secret counsel of evil doers, From the tumult of workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who sharpened as a sword their tongue, They directed their arrow -- a bitter word.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To shoot in secret places the perfect, Suddenly they shoot him, and fear not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They strengthen for themselves an evil thing, They recount of the hiding of snares, They have said, 'Who doth look at it?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They search out perverse things, 'We perfected a searching search,' And the inward part of man, and the heart [are] deep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God doth shoot them [with] an arrow, Sudden have been their wounds,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they cause him to stumble, Against them [is] their own tongue, Every looker on them fleeth away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all men fear, and declare the work of God, And His deed they have considered wisely.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The righteous doth rejoice in Jehovah, And hath trusted in Him, And boast themselves do all the upright of heart!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm of David. A Song. To Thee, silence -- praise, O God, [is] in Zion, And to Thee is a vow completed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hearer of prayer, to Thee all flesh cometh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Matters of iniquities were mightier than I, Our transgressions -- Thou dost cover them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of [him whom] Thou choosest, And drawest near, he inhabiteth Thy courts, We are satisfied with the goodness of Thy house, Thy holy temple.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By fearful things in righteousness Thou answerest us, O God of our salvation, The confidence of all far off ends of earth and sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Establishing mountains by His power, He hath been girded with might,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Restraining the noise of seas, the noise of their billows, And the multitude of the peoples.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the inhabitants of the uttermost parts From Thy signs are afraid, The outgoings of morning and evening Thou causest to sing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast inspected the earth, and waterest it, Thou makest it very rich, the rivulet of God [is] full of water, Thou preparest their corn, When thus Thou dost prepare it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Its ridges have been filled, Deepened hath been its furrow, With showers Thou dost soften it, Its springing up Thou blessest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast crowned the year of Thy goodness, And Thy paths drop fatness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Drop do the pastures of a wilderness, And joy of the heights Thou girdest on.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Clothed have lambs the flock, And valleys are covered with corn, They shout -- yea, they sing!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Song, a Psalm. Shout ye to God, all the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Praise ye the honour of His name, Make ye honourable His praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Say to God, 'How fearful [are] Thy works, By the abundance of Thy strength, Thine enemies feign obedience to Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All the earth do bow to Thee, They sing praise to Thee, they praise Thy name.' Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come ye, and see the works of God, Fearful acts toward the sons of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath turned a sea to dry land, Through a river they pass over on foot, There do we rejoice in Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ruling by His might to the age, His eyes among the nations do watch, The refractory exalt not themselves. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bless, ye peoples, our God, And sound the voice of His praise,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath placed our soul in life, And suffered not our feet to be moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast tried us, O God, Thou hast refined us as the refining of silver.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast brought us into a net, Thou hast placed pressure on our loins.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast caused man to ride at our head. We have entered into fire and into water, And Thou bringest us out to a watered place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I enter Thy house with burnt-offerings, I complete to Thee my vows,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For opened were my lips, And my mouth spake in my distress:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Burnt-offerings of fatlings I offer to Thee, With perfume of rams, I prepare a bullock with he-goats.' Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come, hear, all ye who fear God, And I recount what he did for my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Unto Him [with] my mouth I have called, And exaltation [is] under my tongue.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Iniquity, if I have seen in my heart, The Lord doth not hear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But God hath heard, He hath attended to the voice of my prayer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] God, Who hath not turned aside my prayer, And His loving-kindness, from me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, with stringed instruments. -- A Psalm, a Song. God doth favour us and bless us, Doth cause His face to shine with us. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the knowledge in earth of Thy way, among all nations of Thy salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Praise Thee do peoples, O God, Praise Thee do peoples, all of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rejoice and sing do nations, For Thou judgest peoples uprightly, And peoples on earth comfortest. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Confess Thee do peoples, O God, Confess Thee do peoples -- all of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Earth hath given her increase, God doth bless us -- our God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God doth bless us, and all ends of earth fear Him!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- A Psalm, a song of David. Rise doth God -- scattered are His enemies! And those hating Him flee from His face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the driving away of smoke Thou drivest away, As the melting of wax before fire, The wicked perish at the presence of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the righteous rejoice, they exult before God, And they joy with gladness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sing ye to God -- praise His name, Raise up a highway for Him who is riding in deserts, In Jah [is] His name, and exult before Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Father of the fatherless, and judge of the widows, [Is] God in His holy habitation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God -- causing the lonely to dwell at home, Bringing out bound ones into prosperity, Only -- the refractory have inhabited a dry place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, in Thy going forth before Thy people, In Thy stepping through the wilderness, Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The earth hath shaken, Yea, the heavens have dropped before God, This Sinai -- before God, the God of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A shower of free-will gifts thou shakest out, O God. Thine inheritance, when it hath been weary, Thou hast established it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy company have dwelt in it, Thou preparest in Thy goodness for the poor, O God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Lord doth give the saying, The female proclaimers [are] a numerous host.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Kings of hosts flee utterly away, And a female inhabitant of the house apportioneth spoil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though ye do lie between two boundaries, Wings of a dove covered with silver, And her pinions with yellow gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When the Mighty spreadeth kings in it, It doth snow in Salmon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A hill of God [is] the hill of Bashan, A hill of heights [is] the hill of Bashan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
