translations.text: Why do ye envy, O high hills, The hill God hath desired for His seat? Jehovah also doth tabernacle for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The chariots of God [are] myriads, thousands of changes, The Lord [is] among them, in Sinai, in the sanctuary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast ascended on high, Thou hast taken captive captivity, Thou hast taken gifts for men, That even the refractory may rest, O Jah God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] the Lord, day by day He layeth on us. God Himself [is] our salvation. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God Himself [is] to us a God for deliverances, And Jehovah Lord hath the outgoings of death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- God doth smite The head of His enemies, The hairy crown of a habitual walker in his guilt.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Lord said: 'From Bashan I bring back, I bring back from the depths of the sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that thou dashest thy foot in blood, [In the blood of] enemies -- the tongue of Thy dogs.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have seen Thy goings, O God, Goings of my God, my king, in the sanctuary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Singers have been before, Behind [are] players on instruments, In the midst virgins playing with timbrels.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In assemblies bless ye God, The Lord -- from the fountain of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There [is] little Benjamin their ruler, Heads of Judah their defence, Heads of Zebulun -- heads of Naphtali.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy God hath commanded thy strength, Be strong, O God, this Thou hast wrought for us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of Thy temple at Jerusalem, To Thee do kings bring a present.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rebuke a beast of the reeds, a company of bulls, With calves of the peoples, Each humbling himself with pieces of silver, Scatter Thou peoples delighting in conflicts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come do fat ones out of Egypt, Cush causeth her hands to run to God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Kingdoms of the earth, sing ye to God, Praise ye the Lord. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To him who is riding on the heavens of the heavens of old, Lo, He giveth with His voice a strong voice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ascribe ye strength to God, Over Israel [is] His excellency, and His strength in the clouds.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fearful, O God, out of Thy sanctuaries, The God of Israel Himself, Giving strength and might to the people. Blessed [is] God!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Lilies,' by David. Save me, O God, for come have waters unto the soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have sunk in deep mire, And there is no standing, I have come into the depths of the waters, And a flood hath overflown me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been wearied with my calling, Burnt hath been my throat, Consumed have been mine eyes, waiting for my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those hating me without cause Have been more than the hairs of my head, Mighty have been my destroyers, My lying enemies, That which I took not away -- I bring back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, Thou -- Thou hast known Concerning my overturn, And my desolations from Thee have not been hid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not those waiting on Thee be ashamed because of me, O Lord, Jehovah of Hosts, Let not those seeking Thee Blush because of me, O God of Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For because of Thee I have borne reproach, Shame hath covered my face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A stranger I have been to my brother, And a foreigner to sons of my mother.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For zeal for Thy house hath consumed me, And the reproaches of Thy reproachers Have fallen upon me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I weep in the fasting of my soul, And it is for a reproach to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I make my clothing sackcloth, And I am to them for a simile.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those sitting at the gate meditate concerning me, And those drinking strong drink, Play on instruments.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- my prayer [is] to Thee, O Jehovah, A time of good pleasure, O God, In the abundance of Thy kindness, Answer me in the truth of Thy salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Deliver me from the mire, and let me not sink, Let me be delivered from those hating me, And from deep places of waters.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not a flood of waters overflow me, Nor let the deep swallow me up, Nor let the pit shut her mouth upon me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Answer me, O Jehovah, for good [is] Thy kindness, According to the abundance Of Thy mercies turn Thou unto me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And hide not Thy face from Thy servant, For I am in distress -- haste, answer me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be near unto my soul -- redeem it, Because of mine enemies ransom me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou -- Thou hast known my reproach, And my shame, and my blushing, Before Thee [are] all mine adversaries.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Reproach hath broken my heart, and I am sick, And I look for a bemoaner, and there is none, And for comforters, and I have found none.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they give for my food gall, And for my thirst cause me to drink vinegar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their table before them is for a snare, And for a recompence -- for a trap.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Darkened are their eyes from seeing, And their loins continually shake Thou.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pour upon them Thine indignation, And the fierceness of Thine anger doth seize them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their tower is desolated, In their tents there is no dweller.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they have pursued him Thou hast smitten, And recount of the pain of Thy pierced ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give punishment for their iniquity, And they enter not into Thy righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are blotted out of the book of life, And with the righteous are not written.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I [am] afflicted and pained, Thy salvation, O God, doth set me on high.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I praise the name of God with a song, And I magnify Him with thanksgiving,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is better to Jehovah than an ox, A bullock -- horned -- hoofed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The humble have seen -- they rejoice, Ye who seek God -- and your heart liveth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Jehovah hearkeneth unto the needy, And His bound ones He hath not despised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The heavens and earth do praise Him, Seas, and every moving thing in them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For God doth save Zion, And doth build the cities of Judah, And they have dwelt there, and possess it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the seed of His servants inherit it, And those loving His name dwell in it!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, by David. -- 'To cause to remember.' O God, to deliver me, O Jehovah, for my help, haste.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let them be ashamed and confounded Who are seeking my soul, Let them be turned backward and blush Who are desiring my evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let them turn back because of their shame, Who are saying, 'Aha, aha.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let all those seeking Thee joy and be glad in Thee, And let those loving Thy salvation Say continually, 'God is magnified.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I [am] poor and needy, O God, haste to me, My help and my deliverer [art] Thou, O Jehovah, tarry Thou not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Thee, O Jehovah, I have trusted, Let me not be ashamed to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Thy righteousness Thou dost deliver me, And dost cause me to escape, Incline unto me Thine ear, and save me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be to me for a rock -- a habitation, To go in continually, Thou hast given command to save me, For my rock and my bulwark [art] Thou.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O my God, cause me to escape From the hand of the wicked, From the hand of the perverse and violent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou [art] my hope, O Lord Jehovah, My trust from my youth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By Thee I have been supported from the womb, From my mother's bowels Thou dost cut me out, In Thee [is] my praise continually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a wonder I have been to many, And Thou [art] my strong refuge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Filled is my mouth [with] Thy praise, All the day [with] Thy beauty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cast me not off at the time of old age, According to the consumption of my power forsake me not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For mine enemies have spoken against me, And those watching my soul have taken counsel together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Saying, 'God hath forsaken him, Pursue and catch him, for there is no deliverer.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, be not far from me, O my God, for my help make haste.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are ashamed, they are consumed, Who are opposing my soul, They are covered [with] reproach and blushing, Who are seeking my evil,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I continually do wait with hope, And have added unto all Thy praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My mouth recounteth Thy righteousness, All the day Thy salvation, For I have not known the numbers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I come in the might of the Lord Jehovah, I mention Thy righteousness -- Thine only.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God, Thou hast taught me from my youth, And hitherto I declare Thy wonders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And also unto old age and grey hairs, O God, forsake me not, Till I declare Thy strength to a generation, To every one that cometh Thy might.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thy righteousness, O God, [is] unto the heights, Because Thou hast done great things, O God, who [is] like Thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because Thou hast showed me many and sad distresses, Thou turnest back -- Thou revivest me, And from the depths of the earth, Thou turnest back -- Thou bringest me up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost increase my greatness, And Thou surroundest -- Thou comfortest me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I also thank Thee with a vessel of psaltery, Thy truth, O my God, I sing to Thee with a harp, O Holy One of Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My lips cry aloud when I sing praise to Thee, And my soul that Thou hast redeemed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My tongue also all the day uttereth Thy righteousness, Because ashamed -- because confounded, Have been those seeking my evil!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By Solomon. O God, Thy judgments to the king give, And Thy righteousness to the king's son.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He judgeth Thy people with righteousness, And Thy poor with judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The mountains bear peace to the people, And the heights by righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He judgeth the poor of the people, Giveth deliverance to the sons of the needy, And bruiseth the oppressor.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They fear Thee with the sun, and before the moon, Generation -- generations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He cometh down as rain on mown grass, As showers -- sprinkling the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Flourish in his days doth the righteous, And abundance of peace till the moon is not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he ruleth from sea unto sea, And from the river unto the ends of earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before him bow do the inhabitants of the dry places, And his enemies lick the dust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Kings of Tarshish and of the isles send back a present. Kings of Sheba and Seba a reward bring near.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all kings do bow themselves to him, All nations do serve him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he delivereth the needy who crieth, And the poor when he hath no helper,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath pity on the poor and needy, And the souls of the needy he saveth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From fraud and from violence he redeemeth their soul, And precious is their blood in his eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he liveth, and giveth to him of the gold of Sheba, And prayeth for him continually, All the day he doth bless him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is a handful of corn in the earth, On the top of mountains, Shake like Lebanon doth its fruit, And they flourish out of the city as the herb of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His name is to the age, Before the sun is his name continued, And they bless themselves in him, All nations do pronounce him happy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed is Jehovah God, God of Israel, He alone is doing wonders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And blessed [is] the Name of His honour to the age, And the whole earth is filled [with] His honour. Amen, and amen!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The prayers of David son of Jesse have been ended.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of Asaph. Only -- good to Israel [is] God, to the clean of heart. And I -- as a little thing, My feet have been turned aside,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As nothing, have my steps slipped, For I have been envious of the boastful,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The peace of the wicked I see, That there are no bands at their death,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And their might [is] firm.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the misery of mortals they are not, And with common men they are not plagued.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore hath pride encircled them, Violence covereth them as a dress.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their eye hath come out from fat. The imaginations of the heart transgressed;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They do corruptly, And they speak in the wickedness of oppression, From on high they speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have set in the heavens their mouth, And their tongue walketh in the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore do His people return hither, And waters of fulness are wrung out to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they have said, 'How hath God known? And is there knowledge in the Most High?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, these [are] the wicked and easy ones of the age, They have increased strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- a vain thing! I have purified my heart, And I wash in innocency my hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I am plagued all the day, And my reproof [is] every morning.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have said, 'I recount thus,' Lo, a generation of Thy sons I have deceived.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I think to know this, Perverseness it [is] in mine eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till I come in to the sanctuaries of God, I attend to their latter end.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, in slippery places Thou dost set them, Thou hast caused them to fall to desolations.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How have they become a desolation as in a moment, They have been ended -- consumed from terrors.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a dream from awakening, O Lord, In awaking, their image Thou despisest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my heart doth show itself violent, And my reins prick themselves,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I am brutish, and do not know. A beast I have been with Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I [am] continually with Thee, Thou hast laid hold on my right hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With Thy counsel Thou dost lead me, And after honour dost receive me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whom have I in the heavens? And with Thee none I have desired in earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Consumed hath been my flesh and my heart, The rock of my heart and my portion [is] God to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, lo, those far from Thee do perish, Thou hast cut off every one, Who is going a whoring from Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- nearness of God to me [is] good, I have placed in the Lord Jehovah my refuge, To recount all Thy works!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An Instruction of Asaph. Why, O God, hast Thou cast off for ever? Thine anger smoketh against the flock of Thy pasture.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember Thy company. Thou didst purchase of old, Thou didst redeem the rod of Thy inheritance, This mount Zion -- Thou didst dwell in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lift up Thy steps to the perpetual desolations, Everything the enemy did wickedly in the sanctuary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Roared have thine adversaries, In the midst of Thy meeting-places, They have set their ensigns as ensigns.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is known as one bringing in on high Against a thicket of wood -- axes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, its carvings together With axe and hatchet they break down,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have sent into fire Thy sanctuary, to the earth they polluted the tabernacle of Thy name,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They said in their hearts, 'Let us oppress them together,' They did burn all the meeting-places of God in the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Our ensigns we have not seen, There is no more a prophet, Nor with us is one knowing how long.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when, O God, doth an adversary reproach? Doth an enemy despise thy name for ever?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why dost Thou turn back Thy hand, Even Thy right hand? From the midst of Thy bosom remove [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God [is] my king of old, Working salvation in the midst of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast broken by Thy strength a sea -[monster], Thou hast shivered Heads of dragons by the waters,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast broken the heads of leviathan, Thou makest him food, For the people of the dry places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast cleaved a fountain and a stream, Thou hast dried up perennial flowings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thine [is] the day, also Thine [is] the night, Thou hast prepared a light giver -- the sun.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast set up all the borders of earth, Summer and winter Thou hast formed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember this -- an enemy reproached Jehovah, And a foolish people have despised Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Give not up to a company, The soul of Thy turtle-dove, The company of Thy poor ones forget not for ever.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look attentively to the covenant, For the dark places of earth, Have been full of habitations of violence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let not the oppressed turn back ashamed, Let the poor and needy praise Thy name,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Arise, O God, plead Thy plea, Remember Thy reproach from a fool all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Forget not the voice of Thine adversaries, The noise of Thy withstanders is going up continually!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'Destroy not.' -- A Psalm of Asaph. -- A Song. We have given thanks to Thee, O God, We have given thanks, and near [is] Thy name, They have recounted Thy wonders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When I receive an appointment, I -- I do judge uprightly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Melted is the earth and all its inhabitants, I -- I have pondered its pillars. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have said to the boastful, 'Be not boastful,' And to the wicked, 'Raise not up a horn.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Raise not up on high your horn, (Ye speak with a stiff neck.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For not from the east, or from the west, Nor from the wilderness -- [is] elevation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But God [is] judge, This He maketh low -- and this He lifteth up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a cup [is] in the hand of Jehovah, And the wine hath foamed, It is full of mixture, and He poureth out of it, Only its dregs wring out, and drink, Do all the wicked of the earth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I -- I declare [it] to the age, I sing praise to the God of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And all horns of the wicked I cut off, Exalted are the horns of the righteous!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer with stringed instruments. -- A Psalm of Asaph. -- A Song. In Judah [is] God known, in Israel His name [is] great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And His tabernacle is in Salem, And His habitation in Zion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There he hath shivered arrows of a bow, Shield, and sword, and battle. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bright [art] Thou, honourable above hills of prey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Spoiled themselves have the mighty of heart, They have slept their sleep, And none of the men of might found their hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From Thy rebuke, O God of Jacob, Both rider and horse have been fast asleep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou, fearful [art] Thou, And who doth stand before Thee, Since Thou hast been angry!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From heaven Thou hast sounded judgment, Earth hath feared, and hath been still,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the rising of God to judgment, To save all the humble of earth. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the fierceness of man praiseth Thee, The remnant of fierceness Thou girdest on.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Vow and complete to Jehovah your God, All ye surrounding him. They bring presents to the Fearful One.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth gather the spirit of leaders, Fearful to the kings of earth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer, for Jeduthun. -- A Psalm of Asaph. My voice [is] to God, and I cry, my voice [is] to God, And He hath given ear unto me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In a day of my distress the Lord I sought, My hand by night hath been spread out, And it doth not cease, My soul hath refused to be comforted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I remember God, and make a noise, I meditate, and feeble is my spirit. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast taken hold of the watches of mine eyes, I have been moved, and I speak not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have reckoned the days of old, The years of the ages.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I remember my music in the night, With my heart I meditate, and my spirit doth search diligently:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the ages doth the Lord cast off? Doth He add to be pleased no more?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath His kindness ceased for ever? The saying failed to all generations?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath God forgotten [His] favours? Hath He shut up in anger His mercies? Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I say: 'My weakness is, The changes of the right hand of the Most High.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I mention the doings of Jah, For I remember of old Thy wonders,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have meditated on all Thy working, And I talk concerning Thy doings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, in holiness [is] Thy way, Who [is] a great god like God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou [art] the God doing wonders. Thou hast made known among the peoples Thy strength,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast redeemed with strength Thy people, The sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The waters have seen Thee, O God, The waters have seen Thee, They are afraid -- also depths are troubled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Poured out waters have thick clouds, The skies have given forth a noise, Also -- Thine arrows go up and down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of Thy thunder [is] in the spheres, Lightnings have lightened the world, The earth hath trembled, yea, it shaketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the sea [is] Thy way, And Thy paths [are] in many waters, And Thy tracks have not been known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast led as a flock Thy people, By the hand of Moses and Aaron!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An Instruction of Asaph. Give ear, O my people, to my law, Incline your ear to sayings of my mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I open with a simile my mouth, I bring forth hidden things of old,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That we have heard and do know, And our fathers have recounted to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We do not hide from their sons, To a later generation recounting praises of Jehovah, And His strength, and His wonders that He hath done.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He raiseth up a testimony in Jacob, And a law hath placed in Israel, That He commanded our fathers, To make them known to their sons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So that a later generation doth know, Sons who are born, do rise and recount to their sons,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And place in God their confidence, And forget not the doings of God, But keep His commands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they are not like their fathers, A generation apostate and rebellious, A generation! it hath not prepared its heart, Nor stedfast with God [is] its spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of Ephraim -- armed bearers of bow, Have turned in a day of conflict.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have not kept the covenant of God, And in His law they have refused to walk,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they forget His doings, And His wonders that He shewed them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before their fathers He hath done wonders, In the land of Egypt -- the field of Zoan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He cleft a sea, and causeth them to pass over, Yea, He causeth waters to stand as a heap.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And leadeth them with a cloud by day, And all the night with a light of fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He cleaveth rocks in a wilderness, And giveth drink -- as the great deep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And bringeth out streams from a rock, And causeth waters to come down as rivers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they add still to sin against Him, To provoke the Most High in the dry place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they try God in their heart, To ask food for their lust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they speak against God -- they said: 'Is God able to array a table in a wilderness?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He hath smitten a rock, And waters flow, yea, streams overflow. 'Also -- bread [is] He able to give? Doth He prepare flesh for His people?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore hath Jehovah heard, And He sheweth Himself wroth, And fire hath been kindled against Jacob, And anger also hath gone up against Israel,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they have not believed in God, Nor have they trusted in His salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He commandeth clouds from above, Yea, doors of the heavens He hath opened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He raineth on them manna to eat, Yea, corn of heaven He hath given to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Food of the mighty hath each eaten, Venison He sent to them to satiety.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He causeth an east wind to journey in the heavens, And leadeth by His strength a south wind,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He raineth on them flesh as dust, And as sand of the seas -- winged fowl,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And causeth [it] to fall in the midst of His camp, Round about His tabernacles.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they eat, and are greatly satisfied, And their desire He bringeth to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have not been estranged from their desire, Yet [is] their food in their mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the anger of God hath gone up against them, And He slayeth among their fat ones, And youths of Israel He caused to bend.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With all this they have sinned again, And have not believed in His wonders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He consumeth in vanity their days, And their years in trouble.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If He slew them, then they sought Him, And turned back, and sought God earnestly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they remember that God [is] their rock, And God Most High their redeemer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And -- they deceive Him with their mouth, And with their tongue do lie to Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And their heart hath not been right with Him, And they have not been stedfast in His covenant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He -- the Merciful One, Pardoneth iniquity, and destroyeth not, And hath often turned back His anger, And waketh not up all His fury.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He remembereth that they [are] flesh, A wind going on -- and it returneth not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How often do they provoke Him in the wilderness, Grieve Him in the desolate place?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, they turn back, and try God, And the Holy One of Israel have limited.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have not remembered His hand The day He ransomed them from the adversary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When He set His signs in Egypt, And His wonders in the field of Zoan,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He turneth to blood their streams, And their floods they drink not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He sendeth among them the beetle, and it consumeth them, And the frog, and it destroyeth them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And giveth to the caterpillar their increase, And their labour to the locust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He destroyeth with hail their vine, And their sycamores with frost,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And delivereth up to the hail their beasts, And their cattle to the burning flames.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He sendeth on them the fury of His anger, Wrath, and indignation, and distress -- A discharge of evil messengers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He pondereth a path for His anger, He kept not back their soul from death, Yea, their life to the pestilence He delivered up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He smiteth every first-born in Egypt, The first-fruit of the strong in tents of Ham.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And causeth His people to journey as a flock, And guideth them as a drove in a wilderness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He leadeth them confidently, And they have not been afraid, And their enemies hath the sea covered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He bringeth them in unto the border of His sanctuary, This mountain His right hand had got,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And casteth out nations from before them, And causeth them to fall in the line of inheritance, And causeth the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they tempt and provoke God Most High, And His testimonies have not kept.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they turn back, And deal treacherously like their fathers, They have been turned like a deceitful bow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And make Him angry with their high places, And with their graven images make Him zealous,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath heard, and sheweth Himself wroth. And kicketh exceedingly against Israel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He leaveth the tabernacle of Shiloh, The tent He had placed among men,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He giveth His strength to captivity, And His beauty into the hand of an adversary,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And delivereth up to the sword His people, And with His inheritance shewed Himself angry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His young men hath fire consumed, And His virgins have not been praised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His priests by the sword have fallen, And their widows weep not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Lord waketh as a sleeper, As a mighty one crying aloud from wine.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He smiteth His adversaries backward, A reproach age-during He hath put on them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He kicketh against the tent of Joseph, And on the tribe of Ephraim hath not fixed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He chooseth the tribe of Judah, With mount Zion that He loved,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And buildeth His sanctuary as a high place, Like the earth, He founded it to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He fixeth on David His servant, And taketh him from the folds of a flock,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From behind suckling ones He hath brought him in, To rule over Jacob His people, And over Israel His inheritance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he ruleth them according to the integrity of his heart, And by the skilfulness of his hands leadeth them!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm of Asaph. O God, nations have come into Thy inheritance, They have defiled Thy holy temple, They made Jerusalem become heaps,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They gave the dead bodies of Thy servants Food for the fowls of the heavens, The flesh of Thy saints For the wild beast of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have shed their blood As water round about Jerusalem, And there is none burying.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: We have been a reproach to our neighbours, A scorn and a derision to our surrounders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when, O Jehovah? art Thou angry for ever? Thy jealousy doth burn as fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pour Thy fury on the nations who have not known Thee, And on kingdoms that have not called in Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For [one] hath devoured Jacob, And his habitation they have made desolate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember not for us the iniquities of forefathers, Haste, let Thy mercies go before us, For we have been very weak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Help us, O God of our salvation, Because of the honour of Thy name, And deliver us, and cover over our sins, For Thy name's sake.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why do the nations say, 'Where [is] their God?' Let be known among the nations before our eyes, The vengeance of the blood of Thy servants that is shed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the groaning of the prisoner come in before Thee, According to the greatness of Thine arm, Leave Thou the sons of death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And turn Thou back to our neighbours, Sevenfold unto their bosom, their reproach, Wherewith they reproached Thee, O Lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we, Thy people, and the flock of Thy pasture, We give thanks to Thee to the age, To all generations we recount Thy praise!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Lilies.' A testimony of Asaph. -- A Psalm. Shepherd of Israel, give ear, Leading Joseph as a flock, Inhabiting the cherubs -- shine forth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before Ephraim, and Benjamin, and Manasseh, Wake up Thy might, and come for our salvation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, cause us to turn back, And cause Thy face to shine, and we are saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah, God of Hosts, till when? Thou hast burned against the prayer of Thy people.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast caused them to eat bread of tears, And causest them to drink With tears a third time.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou makest us a strife to our neighbours, And our enemies mock at it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God of Hosts, turn us back, And cause Thy face to shine, and we are saved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A vine out of Egypt Thou dost bring, Thou dost cast out nations, and plantest it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast looked before it, and dost root it, And it filleth the land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Covered have been hills [with] its shadow, And its boughs [are] cedars of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It sendeth forth its branches unto the sea, And unto the river its sucklings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why hast Thou broken down its hedges, And all passing by the way have plucked it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A boar out of the forest doth waste it, And a wild beast of the fields consumeth it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God of Hosts, turn back, we beseech Thee, Look from heaven, and see, and inspect this vine,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the root that Thy right hand planted, And the branch Thou madest strong for Thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Burnt with fire -- cut down, From the rebuke of Thy face they perish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let Thy hand be on the man of Thy right hand, On the son of man Thou hast strengthened for Thyself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And we do not go back from Thee, Thou dost revive us, and in Thy name we call.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, God of Hosts, turn us back, Cause Thy face to shine, and we are saved!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Gittith.' By Asaph. Cry aloud to God our strength, Shout to the God of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lift up a song, and give out a timbrel, A pleasant harp with psaltery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blow in the month a trumpet, In the new moon, at the day of our festival,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a statute to Israel it [is], An ordinance of the God of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A testimony on Joseph He hath placed it, In his going forth over the land of Egypt. A lip, I have not known -- I hear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the burden his shoulder I turned aside, His hands from the basket pass over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In distress thou hast called and I deliver thee, I answer thee in the secret place of thunder, I try thee by the waters of Meribah. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O My people, and I testify to thee, O Israel, if thou dost hearken to me:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is not in thee a strange god, And thou bowest not thyself to a strange god.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] Jehovah thy God, Who bringeth thee up out of the land of Egypt. Enlarge thy mouth, and I fill it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But, My people hearkened not to My voice, And Israel hath not consented to Me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I send them away in the enmity of their heart, They walk in their own counsels.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that My people were hearkening to Me, Israel in My ways would walk.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a little thing their enemies I cause to bow, And against their adversaries I turn back My hand,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those hating Jehovah feign obedience to Him, But their time is -- to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He causeth him to eat of the fat of wheat, And [with] honey from a rock I satisfy thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: -- A Psalm of Asaph. God hath stood in the company of God, In the midst God doth judge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when do ye judge perversely? And the face of the wicked lift up? Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judge ye the weak and fatherless, The afflicted and the poor declare righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the weak and needy escape, From the hand of the wicked deliver them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They knew not, nor do they understand, In darkness they walk habitually, Moved are all the foundations of earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I have said, 'Gods ye [are], And sons of the Most High -- all of you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But as man ye die, and as one of the heads ye fall,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rise, O God, judge the earth, For Thou hast inheritance among all the nations!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Song, -- A Psalm of Asaph. O God, let there be no silence to Thee, Be not silent, nor be quiet, O God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, lo, Thine enemies do roar, And those hating Thee have lifted up the head,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Against Thy people they take crafty counsel, And consult against Thy hidden ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have said, 'Come, And we cut them off from [being] a nation, And the name of Israel is not remembered any more.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they consulted in heart together, Against Thee a covenant they make,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Tents of Edom, and Ishmaelites, Moab, and the Hagarenes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gebal, and Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia with inhabitants of Tyre,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Asshur also is joined with them, They have been an arm to sons of Lot. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do to them as [to] Midian, As [to] Sisera, as [to] Jabin, at the stream Kishon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They were destroyed at Endor, They were dung for the ground!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Make their nobles as Oreb and as Zeeb, And as Zebah and Zalmunna all their princes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who have said, 'Let us occupy for ourselves The comely places of God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O my God, make them as a rolling thing, As stubble before wind.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a fire doth burn a forest, And as a flame setteth hills on fire,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So dost Thou pursue them with Thy whirlwind, And with Thy hurricane troublest them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fill their faces [with] shame, And they seek Thy name, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are ashamed and troubled for ever, Yea, they are confounded and lost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they know that Thou -- (Thy name [is] Jehovah -- by Thyself,) [Art] the Most High over all the earth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- 'On the Gittith By sons of Korah.' -- A Psalm. How beloved Thy tabernacles, Jehovah of Hosts!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul desired, yea, it hath also been consumed, For the courts of Jehovah, My heart and my flesh cry aloud unto the living God,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (Even a sparrow hath found a house, And a swallow a nest for herself, Where she hath placed her brood,) Thine altars, O Jehovah of Hosts, My king and my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of those inhabiting Thy house, Yet do they praise Thee. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of a man whose strength is in Thee, Highways [are] in their heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those passing through a valley of weeping, A fountain do make it, Blessings also cover the director.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They go from strength unto strength, He appeareth unto God in Zion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, God of Hosts, hear my prayer, Give ear, O God of Jacob. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Our shield, see, O God, And behold the face of Thine anointed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For good [is] a day in Thy courts, O Teacher! I have chosen rather to be at the threshold, In the house of my God, Than to dwell in tents of wickedness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a sun and a shield [is] Jehovah God, Grace and honour doth Jehovah give. He withholdeth not good To those walking in uprightness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah of Hosts! O the happiness of a man trusting in Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the Overseer. -- By sons of Korah. A Psalm. Thou hast accepted, O Jehovah, Thy land, Thou hast turned [to] the captivity of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast borne away the iniquity of Thy people, Thou hast covered all their sin. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast gathered up all Thy wrath, Thou hast turned back from the fierceness of Thine anger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn back [to] us, O God of our salvation, And make void Thine anger with us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the age art Thou angry against us? Dost Thou draw out Thine anger To generation and generation?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost Thou not turn back? Thou revivest us, And Thy people do rejoice in Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Show us, O Jehovah, thy kindness, And Thy salvation Thou dost give to us.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I hear what God Jehovah speaketh, For He speaketh peace unto His people, And unto His saints, and they turn not back to folly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, near to those fearing Him [is] His salvation, That honour may dwell in our land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Kindness and truth have met, Righteousness and peace have kissed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Truth from the earth springeth up, And righteousness from heaven looketh out,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah also giveth that which is good, And our land doth give its increase.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Righteousness before Him goeth, And maketh His footsteps for a way!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Prayer of David. Incline, O Jehovah, Thine ear, Answer me, for I [am] poor and needy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Keep my soul, for I [am] pious, Save Thy servant -- who is trusting to Thee, O Thou, my God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Favour me, O Lord, for to Thee I call all the day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rejoice the soul of Thy servant, For unto Thee, O Lord, my soul I lift up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou, Lord, [art] good and forgiving. And abundant in kindness to all calling Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O Jehovah, my prayer, And attend to the voice of my supplications.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In a day of my distress I call Thee, For Thou dost answer me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is none like Thee among the gods, O Lord, And like Thy works there are none.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All nations that Thou hast made Come and bow themselves before Thee, O Lord, And give honour to Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For great [art] Thou, and doing wonders, Thou [art] God Thyself alone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Show me, O Jehovah, Thy way, I walk in Thy truth, My heart doth rejoice to fear Thy name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I confess Thee, O Lord my God, with all my heart, And I honour Thy name to the age.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thy kindness [is] great toward me, And Thou hast delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O God, the proud have risen up against me, And a company of the terrible sought my soul, And have not placed Thee before them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, O Lord, [art] God, merciful and gracious, Slow to anger, and abundant in kindness and truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look unto me, and favour me, Give Thy strength to Thy servant, And give salvation to a son of Thine handmaid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do with me a sign for good, And those hating me see and are ashamed, For Thou, O Jehovah, hast helped me, Yea, Thou hast comforted me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By sons of Korah. -- A Psalm, a song. His foundation [is] in holy mountains.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah is loving the gates of Zion Above all the tabernacles of Jacob.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Honourable things are spoken in Thee, O city of God. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I mention Rahab and Babel to those knowing Me, Lo, Philistia, and Tyre, with Cush! This [one] was born there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And of Zion it is said: Each one was born in her, And He, the Most High, doth establish her.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Jehovah doth recount in the describing of the peoples, 'This [one] was born there.' Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Singers also as players on instruments, All my fountains [are] in Thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Song, a Psalm, by sons of Korah, to the Overseer, 'Concerning the Sickness of Afflictions.' -- An instruction, by Heman the Ezrahite. O Jehovah, God of my salvation, Daily I have cried, nightly before Thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My prayer cometh in before Thee, Incline Thine ear to my loud cry,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For my soul hath been full of evils, And my life hath come to Sheol.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been reckoned with those going down [to] the pit, I have been as a man without strength.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Among the dead -- free, As pierced ones lying in the grave, Whom Thou hast not remembered any more, Yea, they by Thy hand have been cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast put me in the lowest pit, In dark places, in depths.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Upon me hath Thy fury lain, And [with] all Thy breakers Thou hast afflicted. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast put mine acquaintance far from me, Thou hast made me an abomination to them, Shut up -- I go not forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine eye hath grieved because of affliction, I called Thee, O Jehovah, all the day, I have spread out unto Thee my hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the dead dost Thou do wonders? Do Rephaim rise? do they thank Thee? Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is Thy kindness recounted in the grave? Thy faithfulness in destruction?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Are Thy wonders known in the darkness? And Thy righteousness in the land of forgetfulness?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I, unto Thee, O Jehovah, I have cried, And in the morning doth my prayer come before Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why, O Jehovah, castest Thou off my soul? Thou hidest Thy face from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I [am] afflicted, and expiring from youth, I have borne Thy terrors -- I pine away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Over me hath Thy wrath passed, Thy terrors have cut me off,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have surrounded me as waters all the day, They have gone round against me together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast put far from me lover and friend, Mine acquaintance [is] the place of darkness!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An instruction, by Ethan the Ezrahite. Of the kind acts of Jehovah, to the age I sing, To all generations I make known Thy faithfulness with my mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I said, 'To the age is kindness built, The heavens! Thou dost establish Thy faithfulness in them.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have made a covenant for My chosen, I have sworn to David My servant:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Even to the age do I establish thy seed, And have built to generation and generation thy throne. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and the heavens confess Thy wonders, O Jehovah, Thy faithfulness also [is] in an assembly of holy ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For who in the sky, Compareth himself to Jehovah? Is like to Jehovah among sons of the mighty?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God is very terrible, In the secret counsel of His holy ones, And fearful over all surrounding Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O Jehovah, God of Hosts, Who [is] like Thee -- a strong Jah? And Thy faithfulness [is] round about Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou [art] ruler over the pride of the sea, In the lifting up of its billows Thou dost restrain them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast bruised Rahab, as one wounded. With the arm of Thy strength Thou hast scattered Thine enemies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thine [are] the heavens -- the earth also [is] Thine, The habitable world and its fulness, Thou hast founded them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: North and south Thou hast appointed them, Tabor and Hermon in Thy name do sing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast an arm with might, Strong is Thy hand -- high Thy right hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Righteousness and judgment [Are] the fixed place of Thy throne, Kindness and truth go before Thy face.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O the happiness of the people knowing the shout, O Jehovah, in the light of Thy face they walk habitually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In Thy name they rejoice all the day, And in Thy righteousness they are exalted,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the beauty of their strength [art] Thou, And in Thy good will is our horn exalted,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For of Jehovah [is] our shield, And of the Holy One of Israel our king.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then Thou hast spoken in vision, To Thy saint, yea, Thou sayest, I have placed help upon a mighty one, Exalted a chosen one out of the people,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have found David My servant, With My holy oil I have anointed him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With whom My hand is established, My arm also doth strengthen him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An enemy exacteth not upon him, And a son of perverseness afflicteth him not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have beaten down before him his adversaries, And those hating him I plague,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And My faithfulness and kindness [are] with him, And in My name is his horn exalted.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have set on the sea his hand, And on the rivers his right hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He proclaimeth me: 'Thou [art] my Father, My God, and the rock of my salvation.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I also first-born do appoint him, Highest of the kings of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To the age I keep for him My kindness, And My covenant [is] stedfast with him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have set his seed for ever, And his throne as the days of the heavens.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If his sons forsake My law, And in My judgments do not walk;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If My statutes they pollute, And My commands do not keep,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have looked after with a rod their transgression, And with strokes their iniquity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And My kindness I break not from him, Nor do I deal falsely in My faithfulness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I profane not My covenant, And that which is going forth from My lips I change not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Once I have sworn by My holiness, I lie not to David,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His seed is to the age, And his throne [is] as the sun before Me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the moon it is established -- to the age, And the witness in the sky is stedfast. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou, Thou hast cast off, and dost reject, Thou hast shown Thyself wroth With Thine anointed,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast rejected the covenant of Thy servant, Thou hast polluted to the earth his crown,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast broken down all his hedges, Thou hast made his fenced places a ruin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Spoiled him have all passing by the way, He hath been a reproach to his neighbours,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast exalted the right hand of his adversaries, Thou hast caused all his enemies to rejoice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- Thou turnest back the sharpness of his sword, And hast not established him in battle,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast caused [him] to cease from his brightness, And his throne to the earth hast cast down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast shortened the days of his youth, Hast covered him over [with] shame. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when, O Jehovah, art Thou hidden? For ever doth Thy fury burn as fire?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember, I pray Thee, what [is] life-time? Wherefore in vain hast Thou created All the sons of men?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] the man that liveth, and doth not see death? He delivereth his soul from the hand of Sheol. Selah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Where [are] Thy former kindnesses, O Lord. Thou hast sworn to David in Thy faithfulness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember, O Lord, the reproach of Thy servants, I have borne in my bosom all the strivings of the peoples,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherewith Thine enemies reproached, O Jehovah, Wherewith they have reproached The steps of Thine anointed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Blessed [is] Jehovah to the age. Amen, and amen!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Prayer of Moses, the man of God. Lord, a habitation Thou -- Thou hast been, To us -- in generation and generation,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before mountains were brought forth, And Thou dost form the earth and the world, Even from age unto age Thou [art] God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou turnest man unto a bruised thing, And sayest, Turn back, ye sons of men.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a thousand years in Thine eyes [are] as yesterday, For it passeth on, yea, a watch by night.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast inundated them, they are asleep, In the morning as grass he changeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the morning it flourisheth, and hath changed, At evening it is cut down, and hath withered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For we were consumed in Thine anger, And in Thy fury we have been troubled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast set our iniquities before Thee, Our hidden things at the light of Thy face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For all our days pined away in Thy wrath, We consumed our years as a meditation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Days of our years, in them [are] seventy years, And if, by reason of might, eighty years, Yet [is] their enlargement labour and vanity, For it hath been cut off hastily, and we fly away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who knoweth the power of Thine anger? And according to Thy fear -- Thy wrath?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To number our days aright let [us] know, And we bring the heart to wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn back, O Jehovah, till when? And repent concerning Thy servants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Satisfy us at morn [with] Thy kindness, And we sing and rejoice all our days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cause us to rejoice according to the days Wherein Thou hast afflicted us, The years we have seen evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let Thy work appear unto Thy servants, And Thine honour on their sons.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And let the pleasantness of Jehovah our God be upon us, And the work of our hands establish on us, Yea, the work of our hands establish it!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He who is dwelling In the secret place of the Most High, In the shade of the Mighty lodgeth habitually,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is saying of Jehovah, 'My refuge, and my bulwark, my God, I trust in Him,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He delivereth thee from the snare of a fowler, From a calamitous pestilence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With His pinion He covereth thee over, And under His wings thou dost trust, A shield and buckler [is] His truth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou art not afraid of fear by night, Of arrow that flieth by day,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of pestilence in thick darkness that walketh, Of destruction that destroyeth at noon,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There fall at thy side a thousand, And a myriad at thy right hand, Unto thee it cometh not nigh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But with thine eyes thou lookest, And the reward of the wicked thou seest,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (For Thou, O Jehovah, [art] my refuge,) The Most High thou madest thy habitation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Evil happeneth not unto thee, And a plague cometh not near thy tent,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For His messengers He chargeth for thee, To keep thee in all thy ways,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On the hands they bear thee up, Lest thou smite against a stone thy foot.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On lion and asp thou treadest, Thou trampest young lion and dragon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because in Me he hath delighted, I also deliver him -- I set him on high, Because he hath known My name.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth call Me, and I answer him, I [am] with him in distress, I deliver him, and honour him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With length of days I satisfy him, And I cause him to look on My salvation!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A Psalm. -- A Song for the sabbath-day. Good to give thanks to Jehovah, And to sing praises to Thy name, O Most High,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To declare in the morning Thy kindness, And Thy faithfulness in the nights.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On ten strings and on psaltery, On higgaion, with harp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou hast caused me to rejoice, O Jehovah, in Thy work, Concerning the works of Thy hands I sing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How great have been Thy works, O Jehovah, Very deep have been Thy thoughts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A brutish man doth not know, And a fool understandeth not this; --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When the wicked flourish as a herb, And blossom do all workers of iniquity -- For their being destroyed for ever and ever!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou [art] high to the age, O Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, lo, Thine enemies, O Jehovah, For, lo, Thine enemies, do perish, Separate themselves do all workers of iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou exaltest as a reem my horn, I have been anointed with fresh oil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And mine eye looketh on mine enemies, Of those rising up against me, The evil doers, do mine ears hear.
